Parlour Music@@客厅音乐@@@Early form of light, sentimental popular music; popular in USA and Europe from the late 1700s to the 1870s.@@@早期形式的轻松、感伤流行音乐；18世纪末至19世纪70年代流行于美国和欧洲。

Classical Music_Misc@@古典音乐_其它@@@Part of a broad generalization about the structure and function of music throughout history across the globe, describing traditions distinct from Traditional Folk Music and popular music.@@@这是对全球历史上音乐结构和功能的广泛概括的一部分，描述了区别于传统民间音乐和流行音乐的音乐传统。
Buganda Royal Court Music@@布干达宫廷音乐@@@Highest traditional and refined musical forms of Buganda, reserved for the Kabaka (the king of Buganda).@@@布干达最高雅和精炼的传统音乐形式，专为卡巴卡（布干达国王）保留。
East Asian Classical Music@@东亚古典音乐@@@Covers all the forms of pan-East Asian Classical Music sharing origins in Chinese Classical Music.@@@涵盖所有源自中国古典音乐的泛东亚古典音乐形式。
Inkiranya@@因基兰雅@@@Influential drumming style originating in Burundi carried out by a percussion ensemble.@@@起源于布隆迪的有影响力的鼓乐风格，由打击乐合奏团演奏。
Kete@@克特音乐@@@Traditional percussive style played at special occasions in the courts of high ranking Asante chieftains.@@@在阿散蒂高级酋长宫廷的特殊场合演奏的传统打击乐风格。

Maqāmic Music@@木卡姆音乐@@@Originating in the Middle East and founded upon maqāmāt scales, encompassing an extensive and diverse cultural and musical legacy that spans from Morocco to Southeast Asia.@@@起源于中东，建立在马卡姆音阶之上，涵盖从摩洛哥到东南亚的广泛而多样的文化和音乐遗产。
Minyue@@民乐@@@Modernized composition for Chinese instruments on the basis of Chinese Folk Music, incorporating aspects of Western Classical Music.@@@在中国民间音乐基础上，为中国乐器创作的现代化作品，融入了西方古典音乐的某些方面。
Pìobaireachd@@皮巴洛克@@@Art tradition which originated in the Scottish Highlands, usually associated with the Great Highland bagpipes.@@@起源于苏格兰高地的艺术传统，通常与高地大风笛相关联。
South Asian Classical Music@@南亚古典音乐@@@Meta-genre that covers the diverse forms of Classical Music from South Asia.@@@涵盖南亚各种古典音乐形式的元流派。

Southeast Asian Classical Music@@东南亚古典音乐@@@Formal music developed in Southeast Asian countries (Burma, Cambodia, Thailand, Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, East Timor, Brunei Darussalam) usually in form of gong-chime orchestras such as gamelan, kulintang and pi phat.@@@在东南亚国家（缅甸、柬埔寨、泰国、印度尼西亚、马来西亚、菲律宾、东帝汶、文莱达鲁萨兰国）发展起来的正式音乐，通常以锣琴乐团形式出现，如甘美兰、库林唐和皮帕特。

Tibetan Buddhist Chant@@藏传佛教唱颂@@@Ritual vocal music of the Tibetan Buddhist faith characterized by its low pitch, droning repetitiousness, and frequent use of overtone singing.@@@藏传佛教仪式声乐，以其低沉音调、重复吟诵及频繁运用泛音唱法为特征。
Western Classical Music@@西方古典音乐@@@Broad range of art music associated with the aristocracy that originated in Europe around 500 AD.@@@与贵族阶层相关的广泛艺术音乐，约公元500年起源于欧洲。
March_Misc@@进行曲_其它@@@Strong and regular rhythm originally written to walk in step easier or to back events like parades or marches.@@@强劲而有规律的节奏，最初为便于齐步行进或为游行、阅兵等活动伴奏而创作。
Circus March@@马戏团进行曲@@@Marches composed for use in circuses, one of the most popular forms of entertainment in the early 20th century.@@@为马戏团使用而创作的进行曲，是20世纪初最受欢迎的娱乐形式之一。
Dobrado@@多布拉多进行曲@@@Brazilian musical genre, played by military bands with wooden, brass and also percussive instruments.@@@巴西音乐流派，由军乐队使用木管、铜管及打击乐器演奏。
Funeral March@@葬礼进行曲@@@Slow, solemn marches usually in a minor key; meant to invoke the feeling of a funeral procession.@@@缓慢、庄严的进行曲，通常用小调；旨在唤起送葬队伍行进的感觉。

Marching Band_Misc@@行进乐队_其它@@@Covering the music of Western-derived marching bands, primarily used for entertainment, competition, military, and parade settings, typically involving large-scale orchestras featuring a variety of instruments.@@@涵盖源自西方的军乐队音乐，主要用于娱乐、竞赛、军事和游行场合，通常涉及包含多种乐器的大型管弦乐队。
Beni@@贝尼@@@Festive Zanzibari Marching Band music that emerged in the late 19th century.@@@19世纪末兴起的、喜庆的桑给巴尔军乐队音乐。
Drum and Bugle Corps@@鼓号军乐团@@@Primarily uses drums and bugles, developed from North American military music.@@@主要使用鼓和军号，由北美军乐发展而来。
Drumline@@排鼓队@@@Solely percussion based Marching Band.@@@纯打击乐军乐队。
Fife and Drum Corps@@横笛军鼓队@@@Derived from military field music, primarily uses fifes and drums.@@@源自军事野战音乐，主要使用横笛和鼓。
Guggenmusik@@古根音乐@@@Alemannic type of Marching Band played at carnival events, featuring a cacophonous, seemingly "out of tune" sound.@@@阿勒曼尼式军乐队，在狂欢节活动中演奏，以嘈杂、看似"走调"的声音为特色。
Pep Band@@啦啦队管乐队@@@Performs at athletic events, and typically performs modified versions of Pop songs.@@@在体育赛事中表演，通常演奏流行歌曲的改编版本。

Pipe Band@@风笛队@@@Tradition originating in the Scottish military; standard instruments consist of 6-25 sets of Great Highland bagpipes, 3-10 military snare drums, 1-6 Scottish tenor drums, and one bass drum.@@@源自苏格兰军队的传统；标准乐器包括6-25套高地大风笛、3-10个军用小军鼓、1-6个苏格兰次中音鼓和一个低音鼓。

Worldbeat@@世界节拍@@@Pairs elements from diverse sources from across the globe with contemporary, uplifting Westernised production, created primarily for a Western audience.@@@将来自全球各地的多元元素与当代、积极向上的西式制作相结合，主要为西方观众创作。

Country_Misc@@乡村音乐_其它@@@American genre derived from Southern and Appalachian forms of American Folk Music and later Rock and Pop influences, with a focus on the life and culture of rural America.@@@源自美国南方和阿巴拉契亚地区民间音乐形式，后受摇滚与流行乐影响的美国音乐类型，聚焦美国乡村生活与文化。

Alt-Country@@另类乡村@@@Arose in the 1980s and 1990s, performed by artists with roots in Alternative Rock and Indie Rock who were disconnected from the mainstream Country industry.@@@兴起于20世纪80-90年代，由植根于另类摇滚和独立摇滚、与主流乡村音乐产业脱节的艺术家演绎。

Bluegrass@@蓝草音乐@@@Born in the Appalachian region of the United States during the late 1930s, characterized by fingerpicked banjo along with other traditional instruments.@@@20世纪30年代末诞生于美国阿巴拉契亚地区，以指弹班卓琴及其他传统乐器为特色。
Contemporary Country@@当代乡村@@@Incorporates both heavily Pop-influenced styles, and the more pop-averse movement of Neo-Traditionalist Country.@@@融合深受流行乐影响的风格，以及更抗拒流行的新传统主义乡村音乐运动。

Country & Irish@@乡村与爱尔兰@@@Developed in Ireland; combines North American Country with Irish Folk Music influences, often in the form of traditional Irish songs that have been adapted into country numbers.@@@发展于爱尔兰；融合北美乡村音乐与爱尔兰民谣影响，常表现为改编成乡村曲调的传统爱尔兰歌曲。

Country Boogie@@乡村布吉@@@Uptempo style built around the Boogie Woogie rhythm, with strong beats and prominent electric guitar leads, made popular in the 1940s.@@@以布吉伍吉节奏为基础的快节奏风格，具有强烈的节拍和突出的电吉他主奏，于20世纪40年代流行开来。

Country Folk@@乡村民谣@@@Emphasises Traditional Country instrumentation as well as influences from contemporary Singer-Songwriter traditions, such as a gentler disposition and prominent lyrical storytelling focus.@@@强调传统乡村乐器的运用，同时融入当代唱作人传统的影响，如更温和的曲风和突出的叙事性歌词焦点。
Country Pop@@乡村流行@@@Combination of Country with Pop to appeal to a larger audience.@@@乡村音乐与流行音乐的结合，以吸引更广泛的听众。

Honky Tonk@@酒吧乡村@@@Upbeat style that retains much of the common instrumentation of Traditional Country, as well as featuring prominent guitars (especially the steel guitar), strong backbeats and lyrics commonly about the working-class life.@@@欢快的风格，保留了传统乡村音乐常见的乐器配置，并突出吉他（尤其是钢棒吉他）、强烈的反拍节奏以及通常描绘工薪阶层生活的歌词。

Nashville Sound@@纳什维尔之声@@@Production style characterized by smooth vocals over a relaxed ensemble of guitars and rhythm section, and variously incorporating elements from other styles of Country and Pop.@@@制作风格以流畅的人声为特点，配以轻松的吉他组和节奏部合奏，并不同程度地融合了其他乡村和流行风格的元素。

Progressive Country@@前卫乡村@@@Blend of traditionalist Country styles with elements of Rock, Contemporary Folk, and other countercultural genres; characterized by realistic, self-reflective lyrics and non-traditional songwriting and production elements.@@@将传统主义乡村风格与摇滚、当代民谣及其他反主流文化流派的元素相融合；特点是现实、自省的歌词以及非传统的歌曲创作与制作元素。

Traditional Country@@传统乡村@@@Closely aligned with American Folk Music and Blues roots, from country's inception in Appalachia and the American South until the popularity of Honky Tonk and Country Pop in the 1950s.@@@从乡村音乐在阿巴拉契亚和美国南部的起源，直到20世纪50年代酒馆音乐和乡村流行音乐兴起之前，都与美国民间音乐和蓝调根源紧密相连。

Western@@西部音乐@@@Reflects and portrays the lifestyles of cowboys and other settlers of the Western United States through both Traditional Folk Music and popular song.@@@通过传统民间音乐和流行歌曲，反映并描绘了牛仔和美国西部其他定居者的生活方式。

Western Swing@@西部摇摆@@@Blend of Traditional Country string instrumentation with the driving rhythms of Jazz and regional dance traditions, often prominently featuring fiddle and steel guitar.@@@将传统乡村弦乐器与爵士乐的强劲节奏及地区性舞蹈传统相融合，通常突出小提琴和钢棒吉他的演奏。

Folk_Misc@@民谣_其它@@@Rooted in mostly oral traditions of the peoples of the world, encompassing international Traditional Folk Music as well as Western Contemporary Folk which arose in the 1940s.@@@根植于世界各民族的口头传统，涵盖国际传统民间音乐以及20世纪40年代兴起的西方当代民谣。
Contemporary Folk@@当代民谣@@@Generally acoustic and rooted in modernized Traditional Folk Music, originated with the 20th century American folk revival.@@@通常以原声乐器为主，植根于现代化的传统民间音乐，起源于20世纪的美国民谣复兴运动。

Traditional Folk Music@@传统民谣@@@Informed by a long history of tradition in a specific culture or region, mostly associated with the peasantry and non-aristocratic public.@@@基于特定文化或地区悠久的传统，主要与农民和非贵族阶层相关联。

Reggae / Ska / Dancehall._alias: 雷鬼 / 斯卡 / 舞厅音乐
_tags: Reggae / Ska / Dancehall_Misc@@雷鬼 / 斯卡 / 舞厅音乐_其它@@@Umbrella for related forms of Jamaican Music which have grown beyond their regional roots to become global phenomena.@@@涵盖牙买加音乐的相关形式，这些形式已超越其地域根源，成为全球现象。
Dancehall@@舞厅音乐@@@Developed from Reggae in the late 1970s, when Roots Reggae became more stripped-down, with an increased emphasis on pure rhythm.@@@20世纪70年代末从雷鬼乐发展而来，当时根源雷鬼变得更加精简，更加强调纯粹的节奏。

Reggae@@雷鬼@@@Developed in the 1960s; shuffling "bubble" and offbeat rhythms played on an organ, and staccato guitar and piano chords known as "skank."@@@发展于20世纪60年代；以风琴演奏的摇摆"泡泡"节奏和反拍节奏，以及被称为"斯卡"的断奏吉他和钢琴和弦为特征。

Rocksteady@@稳态摇滚@@@Jamaican popular music that blossomed briefly between 1966 and 1968 as an evolution of Ska, being slower, more relaxed, and soulful.@@@牙买加流行音乐，作为斯卡乐的一种演变，在1966年至1968年间短暂繁荣，节奏更慢、更放松、更富情感。

Ska@@斯卡@@@Originated in Jamaica in the late 1950s, characterized by a signature off-beat rhythm (referred to as the ‘skank’ rhythm), chop-style guitar work, busy bass lines and a use of horn sections.@@@起源于20世纪50年代末的牙买加，以其标志性的反拍节奏（称为"斯卡"节奏）、切分式吉他演奏、密集的贝斯线条以及铜管乐组的使用为特征。
Regional Music_Misc@@地方音乐_其它@@@Music of regional cultural traditions, that is or was predominantly played and produced in these regions.@@@地区文化传统的音乐，主要在这些地区演奏和创作，或曾经如此。
African Music@@非洲音乐@@@All forms with roots in Africa.@@@所有源自非洲的音乐形式。

Ancient Music@@古代音乐@@@Umbrella term encompassing all the genres coming after the Prehistoric Music and before the Medieval music eras from ancient civilizations, roughly before 500 CE, following the Western Classical Music point of view with the date of the Roman Empire fall (476 CE).@@@一个总括性术语，涵盖史前音乐之后、中世纪音乐时代之前（约公元500年前）来自古代文明的所有音乐流派，遵循西方古典音乐以罗马帝国灭亡（公元476年）为节点的观点。
Arabic Music@@阿拉伯音乐@@@Originating with and primarily performed by the Arab people.@@@起源于阿拉伯民族，并主要由其表演。
Asian Music@@亚洲音乐@@@Rooted in the traditions of Asian peoples.@@@植根于亚洲各民族的传统。
Austronesian Music@@南岛音乐@@@Music developed by Austronesian ethnic groups.@@@由南岛语系民族发展起来的音乐。
Caribbean Music@@加勒比音乐@@@Meta-genre covering all forms with roots in the Caribbean region.@@@一个元流派，涵盖所有源自加勒比地区的音乐形式。

Central American Music@@中美洲音乐@@@Music from the Central American region (including Belize, Costa Rica, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Nicaragua, and Panama).@@@来自中美洲地区（包括伯利兹、哥斯达黎加、萨尔瓦多、危地马拉、洪都拉斯、尼加拉瓜和巴拿马）的音乐。
Christian Liturgical Music@@基督教礼仪音乐@@@Traditionally accompanies church services, as well as extra-liturgical functions such as processionals and sermons.@@@传统上用于伴随教堂礼拜仪式，以及游行和布道等非礼仪性活动。
European Music@@欧洲音乐@@@Regional styles with roots in the European continent and surrounding islands.@@@植根于欧洲大陆及周边岛屿的地区性风格。
Hispanic Music@@西班牙语音乐@@@Music of Hispanic America and Spain, and the genres shared between them.@@@西班牙语美洲和西班牙的音乐，以及它们之间共享的音乐流派。
Indigenous American Music@@美洲原住民音乐@@@Diverse styles and genres developed by the indigenous peoples of the Americas.@@@由美洲原住民发展起来的多样化的风格和流派。

Islamic Religious Music & Recitation@@伊斯兰宗教音乐与诵读@@@Music and melodic recitation involved in the Islamic faith, such as the reading of the Qur'an with its melodic component (qira'ah), the adhan call to prayer, and the elements often used in the practice of dhikr (particularly in Sufism).@@@伊斯兰信仰中涉及的音乐和旋律吟诵，例如带有旋律成分的《古兰经》诵读（qira'ah）、宣礼（adhan），以及通常在齐克尔（dhikr，尤其在苏菲主义中）实践中使用的元素。
Jewish Music@@犹太音乐@@@Different Jewish forms coming from different regions of the world, influenced by local cultures.@@@来自世界不同地区、受当地文化影响的各种犹太音乐形式。

Maqāmic Music@@木卡姆音乐@@@Originating in the Middle East and founded upon maqāmāt scales, encompassing an extensive and diverse cultural and musical legacy that spans from Morocco to Southeast Asia.@@@起源于中东，建立在马卡姆音阶之上，涵盖从摩洛哥到东南亚的广泛而多样的文化和音乐遗产。

Northern American Music@@北美音乐@@@Regional styles with roots in Northern America (America north of Mexico, including Canada, United States, Greenland, Saint-Pierre and Miquelon, and Bermuda), typically drawing from European Folk Music and Classical Music as well as West African Music and Indigenous American Music influences.@@@植根于北美洲（墨西哥以北的美洲，包括加拿大、美国、格陵兰、圣皮埃尔和密克隆群岛以及百慕大）的地区性风格，通常融合了欧洲民间音乐、古典音乐以及西非音乐和美洲原住民音乐的影响。
Oceanian Music@@大洋洲音乐@@@Rooted in the traditions of the geographic region of Oceania.@@@植根于大洋洲地理区域的传统。
Polyphonic Chant@@复调唱诵@@@Chanting that combines two or more melodic lines that are harmonically cohesive but differ in rhythm and utilize non-parallel motion.@@@结合两条或以上旋律线的吟唱，这些旋律线和声统一但节奏各异，并采用非平行进行。

Prehistoric Music@@史前音乐@@@Styles from the first cultural periods of the human species (Homo sapiens, as well as certain Homo neanderthalensis). From its birth in the Paleolithic and Neolithic eras to the Ancient Music era beginning around 2000–3000 BC.@@@源自人类物种（智人及部分尼安德特人）最初文化时期的风格。从旧石器时代和新石器时代诞生，延续至约公元前2000-3000年开始的古代音乐时期。
South American Music@@南美音乐@@@Rooted in the traditions of South America.@@@植根于南美洲的传统。
Sufi Music@@苏菲音乐@@@Covering the various musical traditions of Sufis.@@@涵盖苏菲派的各类音乐传统。

Traditional Folk Music@@传统民谣@@@Informed by a long history of tradition in a specific culture or region, mostly associated with the peasantry and non-aristocratic public.@@@基于特定文化或地区悠久的传统历史，主要与农民和非贵族大众相关联。

Turkic-Mongolic Music@@突厥-蒙古音乐@@@Group of related traditions which originated among nomadic people of the Central Asian steppes (speakers of Altaic, i.e. Turkic, Mongolic, and Tungusic languages).@@@起源于中亚草原游牧民族（阿尔泰语系使用者，即突厥语族、蒙古语族和通古斯语族）的一系列相关传统。
Singer-Songwriter_Misc@@创作歌手_其它@@@Heavily focused on lyrics and songwriting, with musical accompaniment tending to take lower precedence.@@@极度注重歌词和歌曲创作，音乐伴奏往往处于次要地位。
Avtorskaya pesnya@@作者之歌@@@Originating in the Soviet Union taking cues from forms of Russian Romance and Russian poetry with the main focus on lyrics.@@@起源于苏联，借鉴了俄罗斯浪漫曲和俄罗斯诗歌的形式，主要焦点在于歌词。

Canzone d'autore@@作者歌曲@@@Music of the Italian Singer-Songwriters that emerged in the 1960s and early 1970s, initially influenced by French Chanson with the addition of uniquely Italian sensibilities.@@@意大利创作歌手音乐，兴起于20世纪60年代至70年代初，最初受法国香颂影响，并融入了独特的意大利情感。
Chanson à texte@@文字香颂@@@French-language songs emphasising sophisticated and poetic lyricism.@@@强调精致诗意歌词的法语歌曲。
Euskal kantagintza berria@@巴斯克新歌@@@Basque genre born during the 1960s inspired by Nova can?ó and Chanson.@@@巴斯克音乐类型，诞生于20世纪60年代，受新歌运动和香颂启发。
Kleinkunst@@小型艺术@@@Dutch-language genre from the Netherlands and Flanders with influences from Cabaret, protest songs, Chanson, and folk.@@@来自荷兰和佛兰德地区的荷兰语音乐类型，受卡巴莱、抗议歌曲、香颂和民谣影响。
Liedermacher@@歌曲作者@@@German style having its origins in literature and theatre rather than American forms like Blues or Rock.@@@德国风格，起源于文学和戏剧，而非布鲁斯或摇滚等美国形式。

Música de interven??o@@干预音乐@@@Wave of Portuguese singer-songwriters in the 1960s and 1970s who agitated against the Estado Novo regime and the colonial war waged by Portugal against national liberation fronts.@@@20世纪60至70年代葡萄牙创作歌手浪潮，他们反对新国家政权以及葡萄牙对民族解放阵线发动的殖民战争。

Nova can?ó@@加泰罗尼亚新歌@@@Arose in Catalonia during the Franco dictatorship, sung in Catalan as a way to resist restrictions placed on regional languages in Spain and retain a regional identity.@@@佛朗哥独裁时期兴起于加泰罗尼亚，用加泰罗尼亚语演唱，以此抵制西班牙对地区语言的限制，并保留地区身份认同。
Nueva canción@@新歌运动@@@Latin American (Nueva canción latinoamericana) and Spanish (Nueva canción espa?ola) movement during the 1960s and 1970s.@@@20世纪60至70年代的拉丁美洲（拉丁美洲新歌）和西班牙（西班牙新歌）运动。

Nòva can?on@@奥克新歌@@@Arose in the Occitania region of southern France, sung in the Occitan language as a way to preserve their culture and resist forced usage of French.@@@兴起于法国南部的奥克西塔尼亚地区，用奥克语演唱，以此保护其文化并抵制强制使用法语。
Poezja ?piewana@@演唱诗歌@@@Polish sung poetry; the poem is the centerpiece of a song, often with sparse instrumental accompaniment.@@@波兰歌唱诗歌；诗歌是歌曲的核心，通常配以稀疏的器乐伴奏。

Trova@@游吟@@@One of the most ancient expressions of Cuban Music, based around lone Singer-Songwriters who use nothing but a guitar as their preferred accompaniment and place emphasis on lyrics.@@@古巴音乐最古老的表达形式之一，以独唱歌手为核心，仅用吉他作为首选伴奏，并强调歌词。

Beatboxing@@节奏口技@@@Form of vocal percussion developed during the 1980s, primarily involving the art of producing drum beats, rhythm, and musical sounds using one's mouth, lips, tongue, voice, and more.@@@一种在20世纪80年代发展起来的人声打击乐形式，主要涉及用嘴、唇、舌、嗓音等制造鼓点、节奏和音乐声音的艺术。

Hip Hop_Misc@@嘻哈_其它@@@Emerged primarily on the United States East Coast in African-American communities in the late 1970s; emphasizes rhythmic beat patterns and a type of spoken vocal delivery known as rapping.@@@主要于20世纪70年代末在美国东海岸的非裔美国人社区兴起；强调节奏感强烈的节拍模式和一种被称为说唱的念白式演唱。

Abstract Hip Hop@@抽象嘻哈@@@Uses much more abstract lyricism than what is considered conventional within the genre, typically through use of metaphors, symbolism, and cryptic meanings.@@@使用比该流派常规作品抽象得多的歌词，通常通过隐喻、象征和隐晦的含义来实现。

Afro Trap@@非洲陷阱@@@Centered mostly in France, features production influenced by modern styles developed in West and Central Africa with Trap-styled vocal flows.@@@主要流行于法国，其制作风格受西非和中非现代风格影响，并带有陷阱音乐式的说唱流。

Afroswing@@非洲摇摆@@@Emerged in the United Kingdom during the mid-2010s, incorporating influences from Dancehall, Afrobeats, Contemporary R&B, and a variety of West African Music.@@@于2010年代中期在英国兴起，融合了舞厅音乐、非洲节拍、当代节奏布鲁斯以及多种西非音乐的影响。
Arabesque Rap@@阿拉伯式说唱@@@Emerged from Turkey in the late 2000s, based almost exclusively around Arabesk samples and featuring aggressive rap vocals.@@@于2000年代末从土耳其兴起，几乎完全基于阿拉伯风情采样，并伴有极具攻击性的说唱人声。

Bongo Flava@@邦戈弗拉瓦@@@Tanzanian genre originating in the mid-1990s in the Dar es Salaam area, combining the influence of Rhythm & Blues, Afrobeat, and Dancehall with native Tanzanian styles like Taarab and Muziki wa dansi.@@@坦桑尼亚音乐流派，起源于1990年代中期达累斯萨拉姆地区，融合了节奏布鲁斯、非洲节拍和舞厅音乐的影响，并结合了本土的塔拉布音乐和舞厅音乐风格。
Boom Bap@@碰击说唱@@@Production style identified by an acoustic-sounding drum pattern of a kick drum on the downbeat followed by a cracking snare on the upbeat.@@@一种制作风格，其特点是使用原声鼓点模式：强拍上为底鼓，弱拍上为清脆的军鼓。

Chipmunk Soul@@花栗鼠灵魂乐@@@Sped-up vocal samples and slick, soulful production.@@@加速的人声采样和流畅、充满灵魂的制作。

Christian Hip Hop@@基督教嘻哈@@@Employs lyrics with a pro-Christianity message to profess the performer's faith, often to attract attention from new potential converts.@@@运用带有亲基督教信息的歌词来宣示表演者的信仰，通常旨在吸引潜在新皈依者的注意。

Cloud Rap@@云说唱@@@Emerged in the early 2010s, known for its relaxed, dream-like, and reverb-heavy production style, taking influence from Chillwave, and producers' obscure sample choices.@@@兴起于2010年代初期，以其轻松、梦幻、混响强烈的制作风格而闻名，受寒潮音乐影响，并采用制作人独特的采样选择。
Comedy Rap@@喜剧说唱@@@Primarily intended to be humorous, through the use of comedic lyrics, production, delivery, or other musical aspects.@@@主要通过喜剧性的歌词、制作、演唱方式或其他音乐元素，旨在达到幽默效果。
Conscious Hip Hop@@意识嘻哈@@@Artists whose lyrics deal with social issues.@@@歌词涉及社会议题的艺术家。
Country Rap@@乡村说唱@@@Combines the flow, production-emphasis, and demeanor of Hip Hop with the lyrical content and general approach of Country.@@@将嘻哈音乐的节奏流、制作重点和态度，与乡村音乐的歌词内容和总体风格相结合。

Crunk@@旷课乐@@@Originating from the southern United States and reliant on drum machines and synthesizer stabs to create an energetic, often dance-oriented sound.@@@起源于美国南部，依赖鼓机和合成器切分音来创造一种充满活力、通常以舞蹈为导向的声音。
Crunkcore@@旷课核@@@Combines rapping, screaming and autotune with synthesizers and Hip Hop beats often influenced by Crunk.@@@将说唱、嘶吼和自动调音与合成器及受旷克音乐影响的嘻哈节拍相结合。

Detroit Sound@@底特律之声@@@Upbeat 808 percussion; simple, funky mallet instruments, keys, strings, and synth bass; and street-centric, often punched-in rapping.@@@轻快的808打击乐；简单、时髦的槌击乐器、键盘、弦乐和合成贝斯；以及街头风格、常带冲击感的饶舌。

Digicore@@数字核@@@Developed in the late 2010s and characterized by frantic, bombastic, and glamourous production, usually derived from Trap, that serves to accompany highly melodious, auto-tuned, and hi-pitched vocal performances, often done with breathy voice singing and sing-rapping.@@@发展于2010年代末，以狂乱、夸张和华丽（通常源自陷阱音乐）的制作风格为特征，用以伴奏高度旋律化、经过自动调音和高音调的人声表演，常伴有气声演唱和唱饶舌。

Dirty South@@脏南嘻哈@@@Originated in the Southern US during the mid-1990s, defined by a bouncy rhythm, fast-tempo snares, 808s, heavy bass, and often rapped in a rhythmic delivery consisting of explicit lyrics.@@@起源于1990年代中期美国南部，以弹性节奏、快节奏军鼓、808鼓、重低音为特征，通常以包含露骨歌词的节奏化说唱方式呈现。
Disco Rap@@迪斯科说唱@@@Most prevalent from 1979 to 1982, featuring MCs rapping over popular Disco breaks.@@@在1979年至1982年间最为盛行，特点是MC在流行的迪斯科间奏上进行说唱。

Drill@@钻头舞曲@@@Originated in Chicago in the early 2010s; broadly characterized by complex, energetic rhythms coupled with gang-oriented, street-conscious, and violent lyrics.@@@起源于2010年代初的芝加哥；广义上以复杂、充满活力的节奏，搭配帮派导向、街头意识和暴力歌词为特征。

Drumless@@无鼓点嘻哈@@@Stripped-down production style pioneered in the early 2010s and identified by a lack of drums or minimal, subtle percussion left untouched from the original sample or filtered out.@@@开创于2010年代初的极简制作风格，其特点是没有鼓点，或仅有源自原始采样、未经改动或被过滤掉的、极简且微妙的打击乐。

Emo Rap@@情绪说唱@@@Moody, melancholic production combining atmospheric Cloud Rap, and Trap's typical drum programming, with sampling from Alternative Rock, Midwest Emo, Emo-Pop, and Pop Punk.@@@忧郁、感伤的制作风格，融合了氛围云说唱和陷阱音乐的典型鼓点编程，并从另类摇滚、中西部情绪摇滚、情绪流行和流行朋克中采样。

Experimental Hip Hop@@实验嘻哈@@@Incorporates experimental and eccentric elements considered unconventional or unorthodox compared to more traditional hip hop.@@@融合了实验性和古怪的元素，相较于更传统的嘻哈音乐，这些元素被认为是非传统或非正统的。
G-Funk@@G-放克@@@Relaxed and organic sound featuring live instrumentation, use of P-Funk samples, and high-pitched synth lines.@@@轻松而有机的声音，以现场乐器演奏、使用P-Funk采样和高音合成器旋律线为特色。
Genge@@更吉@@@Combines elements of Dancehall with various traditions of Kenya.@@@融合了舞厅音乐与肯尼亚的各种传统音乐元素。

Hardcore Hip Hop@@硬核嘻哈@@@Confrontation and aggression, whether in the lyricism, vocal delivery, hard beats, heavy production, or any combination thereof.@@@对抗性和侵略性，无论是体现在歌词、人声演绎、硬朗的节拍、厚重的制作，还是这些元素的任意组合中。
Hipco@@Hipco@@@Developed in Liberia, often featuring conscious and political lyrics and influences from local dance styles.@@@发展于利比里亚，通常以具有社会意识和政治性的歌词为特色，并受到当地舞蹈风格的影响。
Hiplife@@Hiplife@@@Fusion of Ghanaian Highlife with Hip Hop.@@@加纳海莱夫音乐与嘻哈音乐的融合。

Houston Sound@@休斯顿之声@@@Features slow to medium tempos, heavy bass, prominent organs and horns, and live instruments such as keyboards, bass guitar and wah-wah pedal.@@@以中慢速节奏、重低音、突出的风琴和铜管乐，以及键盘、贝斯吉他和哇音踏板等现场乐器为特色。

Hyphy@@海菲@@@Party-focused style with upbeat tempos, heavy bass and prominent synths, all underpinned by a distinctive West Coast-style rhythm, claps on beats 2 and 4, and a strong kick drum on beats 1, 2, and either 3 or 4.@@@以派对为核心风格，节奏明快，重低音突出，合成器音效显著，整体建立在独特的西海岸节奏基础上，第二和第四拍加入击掌声效，第一、二拍及第三或第四拍配有强劲的底鼓。

Instrumental Hip Hop@@器乐嘻哈@@@Can take the form of instrumental versions of songs that normally feature rapping, or hip hop originally released and always intended to be instrumental.@@@可表现为原曲含说唱部分的器乐改编版本，或最初发行时即设计为纯器乐的嘻哈作品。

Jerk@@混蛋舞@@@Sparse, snare-heavy drum patterns loosely inspired by Jerk Rap, which take major elements from other prominent 2010s and 2020s underground hip hop genres like Plugg.@@@受Jerk Rap松散启发的稀疏且军鼓突出的鼓点编排，大量借鉴2010至2020年代其他重要地下嘻哈流派（如Plugg）的核心元素。

Jersey Club Rap@@泽西俱乐部说唱@@@Incorporates Jersey Club's distinct kick-heavy rhythms, which are modified to better suit rapping, featuring less sampling and more atmospheric production than earlier Jersey club.@@@融合泽西俱乐部标志性的重底鼓节奏，经调整更适配说唱表演，相比早期泽西俱乐部风格，采样减少，氛围化制作增强。
Jigg@@吉格@@@Club-oriented genre characterized by heavy 808-driven beats, imposing synthesized brasses and an uptempo rhythm.@@@以俱乐部为导向的流派，特征包括厚重的808鼓机驱动节拍、气势恢宏的合成铜管音色以及快节奏韵律。
Jook@@朱克@@@Fast-paced; draws influence from Bounce, Miami Bass, Jersey Club and Dancehall.@@@节奏迅捷；受Bounce、迈阿密贝斯、泽西俱乐部和舞厅音乐影响。

Lo-Fi Hip Hop@@低保真嘻哈@@@Relaxed, laid-back form of Hip Hop, typically instrumental and influenced by Jazz Rap and Downtempo.@@@松弛悠闲的嘻哈形式，通常为器乐作品，受爵士说唱和缓拍音乐影响。
Lowend@@低频嘻哈@@@Rhythms driven by steady eighth-note digital handclaps, rattling bass, fast tempos, upbeat sound, and laid-back flows.@@@节奏由稳定的八分音符电子击掌声、震颤低音、快速节拍、昂扬音效和慵懒的韵律流动共同驱动。
Miami Bass@@迈阿密贝斯@@@Sustained Roland TR-808 kick drums and faster tempos, along with a distinct Electro influence.@@@持续延展的Roland TR-808底鼓音色与更快节奏，兼具鲜明的电子乐影响。
Mobb Music@@莫布音乐@@@San Francisco Bay Area production style building on slowed down Funk (often utilizing live elements).@@@旧金山湾区制作风格，建立在降速放克音乐基础上（常运用现场演奏元素）。

Motswako@@莫茨瓦科@@@Mainly rapped in a mixture of Setswana and English, incorporating additional languages and local South African and Botswanan influences, particularly Kwaito.@@@主要以茨瓦纳语和英语混合说唱，融入其他语言及南非与博茨瓦纳本土影响，尤以Kwaito风格为甚。
Nerdcore Hip Hop@@书呆子嘻哈@@@Themes touch on such nerd mainstays as science, computers, and science fiction.@@@主题涉及科学、计算机、科幻等宅文化核心领域。
Nervous Music@@神经紧张音乐@@@Dark, sinister and menacing sound, heavy sub-basses, simple melodies and slow, sparse and slamming drums.@@@阴暗险恶的听觉氛围，厚重的次低音，简约的旋律，缓慢稀疏且具有冲击力的鼓点。
Philly Club Rap@@费城俱乐部说唱@@@Mixes the fast and energetic production of Philly Club with similarly frantic and fast rapping.@@@将费城俱乐部音乐快速激昂的制作风格，与同样狂躁迅捷的说唱flow相结合。
Political Hip Hop@@政治嘻哈@@@Features explicitly political lyrics and themes.@@@以明确的政治性歌词与主题为特色。
Pop Rap@@流行说唱@@@Fuses Hip Hop's rapping and production traits with Pop elements such as melodious vocals and light, catchy tunes.@@@融合嘻哈的说唱与制作特色，加入流行元素如旋律优美的声线和轻快抓耳的曲调。
Ratchet@@Ratchet@@@Club-oriented style defined by a repeated synth melody and a sparse drum pattern often accompanied by a "hey" chant.@@@以重复的合成器旋律和稀疏的鼓点模式为特征的俱乐部风格，常伴有“嘿”的呼喊声。
Sigilkore@@符文核@@@Dark and hyperactive production, featuring rapping laced with layers of effects.@@@黑暗且极具活力的制作风格，说唱部分带有层层叠叠的效果音处理。
Snap@@响指舞曲@@@Sparse production, pounding 808 bass, occasional use of third-beat finger snaps, and simple melodies reminiscent of ringtones.@@@制作稀疏，伴有沉重的808贝斯，偶尔使用第三拍响指声，以及类似手机铃声的简单旋律。
