Trap@@陷阱说唱@@@Originated in Atlanta in the early 2000s; distinctive fast hi-hat sound and heavy bass at moderate tempos.@@@起源于21世纪初的亚特兰大；特点是快速的踩镲声和中速节奏下的沉重贝斯。
Trap shaabi@@陷阱沙比@@@Combining Trap percussion and rhythms with Egyptian Shaabi and especially Mahraganat.@@@将陷阱音乐的打击乐和节奏与埃及沙比音乐，特别是马赫拉加纳特音乐相结合。

Turntablism@@唱盘主义@@@Manipulating and re-appropriating vinyl records using a turntable and DJ mixer in a Hip Hop context, often featuring constant scratching and beat juggling between two or more recordings into original, distinctive tracks.@@@在嘻哈语境下，使用唱盘和DJ混音台对黑胶唱片进行操控和再创作，通常包含持续的搓盘声，并在两个或多个录音之间进行节拍切换，创造出独特新颖的曲目。

R&B_Misc@@节奏蓝调_其它@@@Umbrella of styles covering the most popular forms of Black American music from the late 1940s, when the term Rhythm & Blues first appeared; includes the developments of Soul and Funk in the '60s and '70s, and the Contemporary R&B of the 1980s onward.@@@涵盖自1940年代末"节奏布鲁斯"一词首次出现以来最流行的美国黑人音乐风格的统称；包括1960至1970年代灵魂乐和放克的发展，以及1980年代至今的当代节奏布鲁斯。

Acid Jazz@@酸性爵士@@@Eclectic scene consisting of?influences from?Jazz, Funk, Hip Hop, Soul, and various styles of Dance that gained popularity during?the 1990s.@@@融合了爵士、放克、嘻哈、灵魂乐及各种舞曲风格影响的折衷主义场景，在1990年代流行起来。
Beach Music@@沙滩音乐@@@Danceable 4/4 "blues shuffle" rhythmic structure, vocal harmonies, and moderate tempos.@@@具有可舞动的4/4拍"布鲁斯摇摆"节奏结构、和声演唱以及中等速度。

Blue-Eyed Soul@@蓝眼灵魂乐@@@Usually refers to a Pop or Pop Rock song that contains strong Gospel-like elements, primarily popularized by white American and British singers in the 1960s through the 1980s.@@@通常指包含强烈福音音乐元素的流行或流行摇滚歌曲，主要由1960年代至1980年代的美国及英国白人歌手推广普及。

Boogie@@布吉@@@Continuation of the Disco sound with increased influence from Funk, Synth Funk, and R&B and a slower tempo, often featuring drum machines and filter modulated synthesizers.@@@延续迪斯科风格，并融入更多放克、合成器放克和节奏蓝调的影响，节奏较慢，常使用鼓机和滤波器调制合成器。

Contemporary R&B@@当代R&B@@@Soulful, passionate and melismatic vocals originally derived from popular Soul and Disco, as well as smooth, lush and often synthesized production variously influenced by Hip Hop, Funk, and related strands of Pop.@@@源自流行灵魂乐和迪斯科的深情、富有激情且带有装饰音的演唱，以及受嘻哈、放克及相关流行音乐分支影响，流畅、华丽且常使用合成器制作的伴奏。
Doo-Wop@@嘟·喔普@@@Heavy emphasis on male four-part harmonies and light instrumentation; peaked in popularity in the 1950s.@@@极其注重男声四部和声与轻量级器乐编排；在20世纪50年代达到流行巅峰。

Funk@@放克@@@Danceable, syncopated grooves carried by bass and drums alongside percussive guitar, keyboard, and/or horn parts, with rhythmic emphasis on the downbeat.@@@由贝斯和鼓驱动的可舞动、切分的律动，辅以打击乐式的吉他、键盘和/或铜管乐部分，节奏强调重拍。
New Orleans R&B@@新奥尔良R&B@@@Increased rhythmic complexity with added focus on piano and horns.@@@节奏复杂性增加，并更侧重于钢琴和铜管乐。

Rhythm & Blues@@节奏蓝调@@@Predominantly African American style that retains the defined backbeats, Blues chord progressions/melodies and passionate singing/shouting of Jump Blues, while appealing more to an urban audience with smaller bands and reduced Jazz influence.@@@一种以非裔美国人为主的风格，保留了跳跃蓝调中明确的反拍、蓝调和弦进行/旋律以及充满激情的演唱/呼喊，同时通过更小的乐队规模和减少爵士乐影响，更吸引城市听众。

Soul@@灵魂乐@@@Originated in the African American culture of the 20th century, resulting from the combination of the ecstatic vocals of Gospel with the upbeat instrumentation of Rhythm & Blues, consisting of guitar, bass and percussion.@@@起源于20世纪非裔美国人文化，是福音音乐中狂喜的演唱与节奏蓝调中由吉他、贝斯和打击乐组成的欢快器乐相结合的产物。
Soul Blues@@灵魂蓝调@@@Shares similarities with Soul and incorporates the eponymous soulful inflections of Gospel, Jump Blues and soul into the vocals.@@@与灵魂乐有相似之处，并将同名风格中源自福音音乐、跳跃蓝调和灵魂乐的深情转调融入演唱。

Blues_Misc@@蓝调_其它@@@Originated toward the end of the 19th century in African American communities in the United States, particularly the Deep South; drew on traditional Spirituals and Work Song; highly influential to the whole of Western popular music.@@@起源于19世纪末美国非裔社区，尤其是美国南部腹地；借鉴了传统灵歌和工作歌；对整个西方流行音乐影响深远。
Acoustic Blues@@原声蓝调@@@Developed out of Work Song and Spirituals to become the most prominent early form of Blues.@@@从工作歌和灵歌发展而来，成为早期布鲁斯最突出的形式。

Boogie Woogie@@布吉乌吉@@@Early, uptempo Blues featuring the piano as the main instrument, with soloing and improvisation played over the top of a repetitive eight note bassline.@@@早期、快节奏的布鲁斯，以钢琴为主要乐器，在重复的八度低音线上进行独奏和即兴演奏。

Country Blues@@乡村蓝调@@@Various styles of Blues originating in the rural US South, typically relying on expressive vocal delivery and featuring simple, usually acoustic instrumentation.@@@起源于美国南部乡村的各种布鲁斯风格，通常依赖富有表现力的演唱，并采用简单、通常是原声的乐器配置。
Electric Blues@@电声蓝调@@@Major development in sound and reach of Blues when it became possible to amplify the instruments of small combos.@@@当小型乐队的乐器可以被放大时，布鲁斯在声音和传播范围上的一次重大发展。
Fife and Drum Blues@@横笛与军鼓蓝调@@@Rooted in martial Fife and Drum Corps music, originated among Afro-American communities in Mississippi.@@@植根于军乐笛鼓队的音乐，起源于密西西比州的非裔美国人社区。
Jump Blues@@跳跃蓝调@@@Emerged in the late 1930s with influence from Jazz styles such as Swing and Big Band, featuring fast tempos and raucous performances.@@@出现于20世纪30年代末，受摇摆乐和大乐队等爵士风格影响，特点是快节奏和喧闹的表演。

Piano Blues@@钢琴蓝调@@@Features the piano as a lead instrument performed solo or with a small ensemble, ranging from mid-tempo ballads to fast-paced dance styles.@@@以钢琴作为主奏乐器，可独奏或与小型合奏团配合，涵盖中速民谣到快节奏舞曲风格。
Soul Blues@@灵魂蓝调@@@Shares similarities with Soul and incorporates the eponymous soulful inflections of Gospel, Jump Blues and soul into the vocals.@@@与灵魂乐有相似之处，并将同名福音音乐、跳跃布鲁斯和灵魂乐中富有感染力的转调融入人声演唱。
Vaudeville Blues@@杂耍蓝调@@@Vaudeville incorporating heavy Blues elements.@@@融合了浓厚布鲁斯元素的歌舞杂耍表演音乐。

Jazz_Misc@@爵士乐_其它@@@Originating in African American communities in the Southern United States around the turn of the 20th century, building on New Orleans Brass Band ensemble and influences from Ragtime and Blues to become a very popular style by the emergence of Swing in the 1930s.@@@起源于20世纪初美国南部的非裔美国人社区，以新奥尔良铜管乐队合奏为基础，并受到拉格泰姆和布鲁斯的影响，到20世纪30年代摇摆乐兴起时已成为非常流行的风格。
Afro-Jazz@@非洲爵士@@@African-styled percussion and rhythms.@@@非洲风格的打击乐和节奏。
Arabic Jazz@@阿拉伯爵士@@@Blending of Arabic Music and Jazz.@@@阿拉伯音乐与爵士乐的融合。

Avant-Garde Jazz@@前卫爵士@@@Combines traditional or mainstream Jazz stylings with non-traditional techniques of avant-garde art music by experimenting with characteristics such as rhythm, harmony, structure, melody, and instrumentation.@@@通过实验节奏、和声、结构、旋律和配器等特征，将传统或主流爵士乐风格与先锋派艺术音乐的非传统技巧相结合。
Bebop@@比波普@@@Fast tempos, improvisation, melodic chromaticism, and complex harmonies and chord progressions.@@@快节奏、即兴演奏、旋律半音化以及复杂的和声与和弦进行。

Big Band@@大乐团@@@Commonly associated with the golden era of Swing in the 1930s and 1940s, performed by a large ensemble including brass, woodwinds, and usually a rhythm section.@@@通常与20世纪30至40年代摇摆乐的黄金时代相关，由大型乐团演奏，包括铜管乐器、木管乐器，通常还有一个节奏组。
British Dance Band@@英国舞曲乐队@@@Came from the dancehalls of the 1920s and 1930s, combining a British take on Jazz with the Music Hall tradition.@@@源于20世纪20至30年代的舞厅，将英国式的爵士乐理解与音乐厅传统相结合。
Bulawayo Jazz@@布拉瓦约爵士@@@Emerged from the city of Bulawayo, Zimbabwe in the early 1950s, led by the alto sax and accompanied by ensembles.@@@20世纪50年代初兴起于津巴布韦的布拉瓦约市，以中音萨克斯为主奏，由合奏团伴奏。
Cape Jazz@@开普爵士@@@Developed parallel to American Jazz in the early 20th century, named for its origin in Cape Town, South Africa.@@@与20世纪初的美国爵士乐并行发展，因其起源于南非开普敦而得名。

Cartoon Music@@卡通音乐@@@Zany and uptempo; incorporates elements of Western Classical Music and Modern Classical into frantic Big Band arrangements, playfully chaotic to match the on-screen actions of Cartoon characters and the beat of their movements.@@@古怪而快节奏；将西方古典音乐和现代古典音乐的元素融入狂热的大乐队编曲中，其戏谑的混乱感旨在匹配卡通角色的屏幕动作及其运动节拍。

Chamber Jazz@@室内爵士@@@Made by mostly small acoustic-based ensembles with emphasized improvisation and influenced by Neoclassicism and Traditional Folk Music.@@@主要由小型原声乐器合奏团创作，强调即兴演奏，并受到新古典主义和传统民间音乐的影响。
Cool Jazz@@酷爵士@@@Developed in the late 1940s alongside and in response to Bebop, featuring a more restrained, emotionally "cool" style.@@@20世纪40年代末与比波普同时发展并作为对其的回应，其风格更为克制，情感上"冷静"。

Crime Jazz@@犯罪爵士@@@Gritty, moody style that emerged in the mid-1950s to accompany film noir movies and hard-boiled detective/crime-themed TV shows.@@@一种粗粝、忧郁的风格，于20世纪50年代中期出现，用于为黑色电影和硬汉侦探/犯罪题材的电视节目配乐。
Dark Jazz@@黑暗爵士@@@Melancholic and gloomy Film noir-inspired atmosphere and influence from Crime Jazz and Dark Ambient.@@@忧郁阴沉的黑色电影氛围，并受到犯罪爵士乐和暗氛围音乐的影响。

Dixieland@@迪克西兰爵士@@@Developed in New Orleans in the 1910s, incorporating influences from Ragtime and Blues into cacophonous, polyphonic arrangements with instruments trading places between accompaniment and soloist.@@@起源于1910年代的新奥尔良，融合了拉格泰姆与蓝调的影响，形成一种喧闹、多声部的编曲风格，乐器在伴奏与独奏之间交替转换。

ECM Style Jazz@@ECM风格爵士@@@Primarily associated with ECM Records, combining elements of Jazz Fusion with the subdued atmosphere of Cool Jazz and the Western Classical Music bent of Third Stream.@@@主要与ECM唱片公司关联，融合了爵士融合乐的元素、酷爵士的低调氛围以及第三潮流乐派的西方古典音乐倾向。
Ethio-Jazz@@埃塞俄比亚爵士@@@Unique blend of traditional Ethiopic Music and Jazz.@@@传统埃塞俄比亚音乐与爵士乐的独特融合。
Flamenco Jazz@@弗拉明戈爵士@@@Combination of Jazz with Flamenco nuevo, the contemporary style of Flamenco, which was pioneered in the late 1960s.@@@爵士乐与新弗拉门戈的结合，后者是始于1960年代末的当代弗拉门戈风格。

Hard Bop@@硬波普@@@A continuation of Bebop that reintroduces more traditional African-American musical features, such as bluesy melodies, slow to medium tempos, and a more groove-oriented sound, while retaining the expressive improvisation.@@@比波普的延续，重新引入了更多传统的非裔美国人音乐特征，如蓝调旋律、中慢速节奏和更注重律动的声响，同时保留了富有表现力的即兴演奏。
Indo Jazz@@印度爵士@@@Fuses the various musical traditions of the Indian subcontinent with jazzy musical structures.@@@将印度次大陆的各种音乐传统与爵士乐的音乐结构相融合。

Jazz Fusion@@爵士融合@@@Near-universal usage of electric guitars and keyboards coupled with influences from genres such as Rock, Rhythm & Blues, Funk, and Traditional Folk Music, and a relative degree of harmonic simplicity.@@@几乎普遍使用电吉他和键盘，并受到摇滚、节奏布鲁斯、放克和传统民间音乐等流派的影响，和声相对简单。

Jazz Poetry@@爵士诗歌@@@Emerged in the 1920s, incorporating the style and rhythms of Jazz as both art forms spread throughout African-American communities in the United States.@@@兴起于1920年代，在爵士乐与另一种艺术形式在美国非裔社区传播的过程中，融入了爵士的风格与节奏。
Jazz manouche@@吉普赛爵士@@@Combines Jazz with several aspects of Romani Folk Music.@@@将爵士乐与罗姆民间音乐的多个方面相结合。
Jazz-Funk@@爵士放克@@@Jazz highly influenced by Funk.@@@深受放克音乐影响的爵士乐。
Kréyol djaz@@克里奥尔爵士@@@Combines Jazz with various styles of French Caribbean Music.@@@将爵士乐与法属加勒比地区的多种音乐风格相结合。
Latin Jazz@@拉丁爵士@@@Fusion with Latin American rhythms, especially those of African origin.@@@与拉丁美洲节奏的融合，尤其是那些源自非洲的节奏。
Marabi@@马拉比爵士@@@South African Jazz with origins in the 1920s and rooted in traditional South African music.@@@起源于1920年代并植根于传统南非音乐的南非爵士乐。
Modal Jazz@@调式爵士@@@Utilizes musical modes as harmonic framework over traditionally used chord progressions.@@@使用调式作为和声框架，取代了传统上使用的和弦进行。

Post-Bop@@后波普@@@Grew out of Hard Bop in the early to mid-1960s incorporating certain untraditional elements inspired largely in part from Avant-Garde Jazz, Modal Jazz and eventually, Free Jazz, such as non-diatonic chord progressions, irregular rhythms and amelodic forms.@@@在1960年代早中期从硬波普发展而来，融入了主要受前卫爵士、调式爵士乃至自由爵士启发的非传统元素，如非自然音阶的和弦进行、不规则节奏和非旋律性形式。
Smooth Jazz@@柔顺爵士@@@Simple melodies and slick production influenced by Pop and R&B.@@@受流行乐和R&B影响的简单旋律与精良制作。
Soul Jazz@@灵魂爵士@@@Appeared during the 1950s when Gospel, Rhythm & Blues and later Soul started to influence the style of prolific Jazz musicians.@@@出现于1950年代，当时福音音乐、节奏布鲁斯以及后来的灵魂乐开始影响多产的爵士音乐家的风格。

Spiritual Jazz@@灵性爵士@@@Emerged in the 1960s, marked by a mixture of Jazz with approximations of ethnic styles, religious music of non-Christian traditions, and the ecstatic, transcendental aspects of Free Jazz.@@@兴起于20世纪60年代，特点是将爵士乐与近似民族风格、非基督教传统的宗教音乐以及自由爵士乐的狂喜超验元素相融合。
Spy Music@@间谍音乐@@@Meant to evoke the action and drama of Spy films.@@@旨在唤起间谍电影的动作感和戏剧性。

Stride@@跨步钢琴@@@Early form of Ragtime-derived Jazz with improvisational tendencies; named for the performers' left hand "striding" across the piano keyboard.@@@源自拉格泰姆的早期爵士乐形式，具有即兴倾向；因演奏者左手在钢琴键盘上"跨越"弹奏而得名。

Sweet Jazz@@甜蜜爵士@@@Early form developed by white bandleaders and highly popular with white audiences in the 1920s, emphasizing arrangements and strong melodies with a straighter rhythmic feel, de-emphasizing improvisation compared to "hot" Dixieland.@@@由白人乐队领班发展起来的早期形式，在20世纪20年代深受白人听众欢迎，强调编曲和强烈的旋律，节奏感更直白，与"热辣"的迪克西兰爵士相比，弱化了即兴演奏。
Swing@@摇摆乐@@@Distinctive rhythm and large backing rhythm section over which solos are performed.@@@独特的节奏和庞大的伴奏节奏部，独奏在其上进行。
Third Stream@@第三潮流@@@A fusion of Jazz and Western Classical Music, with improvisation as a vital component.@@@爵士乐与西方古典音乐的融合，即兴演奏是其重要组成部分。
Vocal Jazz@@人声爵士@@@Focused on vocals, generally using a more traditional, poppy strong structure and reducing the role of improvisation.@@@以人声为主，通常采用更传统、流行的歌曲结构，并减少即兴演奏的作用。

West Coast Jazz@@西海岸爵士@@@Laidback style of jazz related aesthetically to Cool Jazz, developed by musicians based in Los Angeles and San Francisco, California in the 1950s.@@@与冷爵士乐美学相关的悠闲爵士风格，由20世纪50年代加利福尼亚州洛杉矶和旧金山的音乐家发展起来。

Ambient Pop@@氛围流行@@@Emphasizes texture and atmosphere with a degree of structural experimentation, drawing on Dream Pop, Neo-Psychedelia, and Post-Rock while tending towards more minimal, repetitive arrangements which may incorporate elements of Electronic or Krautrock.@@@强调质感和氛围，带有一定程度的结构实验性，借鉴梦幻流行、新迷幻和后摇滚，倾向于更简约、重复的编曲，可能融入电子或德国摇滚元素。
Mashup@@混搭@@@Songs created by mixing parts of two or more recordings together.@@@通过将两个或多个录音片段混合在一起而创作的歌曲。
Toypop@@玩具流行@@@Avant-garde, naive, and child-like; explores the sonic capabilities of toy instruments.@@@前卫、天真、童趣；探索玩具乐器的声音可能性。
Ambient_Misc@@氛围音乐_其它@@@Emphasizes texture and tone over traditional musical structure, aimed at evoking a particular atmosphere or mood.@@@强调质感和音色胜过传统音乐结构，旨在唤起特定的氛围或情绪。
Ambient Americana@@美式氛围@@@Made using traditional Americana and Country instrumentation, typically to evoke a pastoral atmosphere.@@@使用传统美式民谣和乡村乐器制作，通常旨在唤起田园氛围。
Dark Ambient@@黑暗氛围@@@Emphasizes an ominous, gloomy, and dissonant atmosphere.@@@强调不祥、阴郁和不和谐的氛围。
Space Ambient@@太空氛围@@@Flowing and relaxing synthesizer-based music derived from Progressive Electronic, often oriented around imagery of outer space.@@@源自前卫电子的流畅、放松的合成器音乐，常以外太空意象为核心。
Tribal Ambient@@部落氛围@@@Combines the theory and atmosphere of Ambient with traditional instruments and sounds from around the globe.@@@将氛围音乐的理念和氛围与来自全球各地的传统乐器和声音相结合。

Dance_Misc@@舞曲_其它@@@Rooted in Disco and developed largely within Electronic Dance Music; emphasizes rhythm and is generally produced for play by DJs at clubs, parties, or festivals.@@@植根于迪斯科，并主要在电子舞曲中发展；强调节奏，通常为DJ在俱乐部、派对或音乐节上播放而制作。

Alternative Dance@@另类舞曲@@@Incorporates rhythms and synthesizers from Electronic Dance Music within the context of Alternative Rock and related styles; predominantly popular in the 1990s.@@@在另类摇滚及相关风格的框架内，融入电子舞曲的节奏和合成器音色；在20世纪90年代尤为流行。
Dance-Pop@@流行舞曲@@@Simple, yet catchy melodies and throbbing beats inspired initially by Disco and later various House styles.@@@简单却朗朗上口的旋律和强劲的节拍，最初受迪斯科启发，后来受各种浩室风格影响。
Disco@@迪斯科@@@Combines the lush orchestration of Philly Soul and bass grooves of Funk with a four-on-the-floor rhythm.@@@将费城灵魂乐的华丽管弦乐编排与放克的贝斯律动相结合，并采用四四拍的节奏。

Electronic Dance Music@@电子舞曲@@@Broad category for subgenres mainly derived from Disco, featuring Electronic sounds, synthesizers, drum machines and varying BPM ranges.@@@一个宽泛的类别，涵盖主要源自迪斯科的子流派，以电子音色、合成器、鼓机和不同的BPM范围为特征。
Miami Bass@@迈阿密贝斯@@@Sustained Roland TR-808 kick drums and faster tempos, along with a distinct Electro influence.@@@持续不断的Roland TR-808底鼓声和更快的节奏，并带有明显的电子乐影响。

Wa Euro@@和风欧洲舞曲@@@Traditional form of J-Pop that emerged in the second half of the 1980s from late Hi-NRG, '80s British Dance-Pop and Italo-Disco/Eurobeat.@@@传统形式的日本流行乐，于1980年代后半期从晚期高能舞曲、80年代英国舞曲流行乐和意大利迪斯科/欧陆节拍中衍生而来。

Darkwave_Misc@@暗潮_其它@@@Expands on the dark, introspective and sorrowful atmosphere of Gothic Rock; borrowing aspects from Electronic and other non-Rock influences.@@@拓展了哥特摇滚黑暗、内省和悲伤的氛围；借鉴了电子乐及其他非摇滚流派的影响。

Ethereal Wave@@仙音@@@Atmospheric guitar and synthesizer soundscapes, effects like reverb and delay, and commonly soprano female vocals to create a surreal, otherworldly effect.@@@氛围化的吉他与合成器音景，混响和延迟等效果，以及通常为女高音的人声，共同营造出一种超现实、超凡脱俗的效果。
Neoclassical Darkwave@@新古典暗潮@@@Combines dark and melancholic atmosphere with Western Classical Music.@@@将黑暗忧郁的氛围与西方古典音乐相结合。

Neue Deutsche Todeskunst@@新德国死亡艺术@@@Dark themes inspired by German philosophy and poetry, typically combining Electronic instrumentation with neoclassical influences and an eccentric, theatrical style.@@@受德国哲学与诗歌启发的黑暗主题，通常将电子乐器与新古典主义影响以及古怪、戏剧化的风格相结合。
Electronic_Misc@@电子音乐_其它@@@Uses non-traditional electronic instrumentation and sound manipulation technology as the primary backbone of a composition.@@@使用非传统的电子乐器和声音处理技术作为乐曲的主要骨架。
Acholitronix@@阿乔利电子@@@Fusion of traditional Acholi rhythms and melodies with Electronic production, originating in the city of Gulu.@@@传统阿乔利节奏和旋律与电子制作的融合，起源于古卢市。
Algorave@@算法锐舞@@@Generative dancefloor-oriented music produced live via algorithms.@@@通过算法现场生成的、面向舞池的生成式音乐。
Binaural Beats@@双耳节拍@@@Auditory illusions created when two different frequencies are played simultaneously in separate ears.@@@当两个不同频率的声音分别在左右耳播放时产生的听觉错觉。

Bit Music@@位元音乐@@@Created by sound chips, cards, and sequencers found in video game consoles, personal computers, and arcade machines, as well as synthesizers emulating these sounds.@@@由电子游戏机、个人电脑、街机中的音效芯片、声卡和音序器，以及模拟这些声音的合成器所创造。

Bitpop@@位元流行@@@Fuses Bit Music with additional synths, beats, guitars and modern production values, emphasizing highly catchy melodies and relatively fast tempos.@@@将比特音乐与额外的合成器音效、节拍、吉他及现代制作水准融合，强调极具感染力的旋律和相对较快的节奏。
Celtic Electronica@@凯尔特电子@@@Fusion of Celtic Folk Music with various types of Electronic Dance Music.@@@凯尔特民谣音乐与各类电子舞曲的融合。
Chillout@@驰放音乐@@@Broad category of many relaxed styles of Electronic music which predominantly draw influence from Electronic Dance Music subgenres and Ambient.@@@涵盖多种轻松风格电子音乐的广泛类别，主要受电子舞曲子流派和氛围音乐影响。

Digital Fusion@@数字融合@@@Incorporates the hardware-limited sounds of Chiptune and other Bit Music into compositions drawing on Jazz, Cinematic Classical, progressive music, and other Electronic styles, sometimes augmented with live instrumentation.@@@将芯片音乐和其他比特音乐中受硬件限制的声音融入借鉴爵士乐、电影古典乐、前卫音乐及其他电子风格的创作中，有时辅以现场乐器演奏。
Drift Phonk@@漂移冯克@@@Busy, noisy production inspired by lo-fi Memphis Rap; frequently features 808 cowbell melodies and chopped, compressed samples.@@@受低保真孟菲斯说唱启发的繁复、嘈杂的制作；常以808牛铃旋律和经过切分、压缩的采样为特色。

Dungeon Synth@@地牢合成器@@@Focuses on evoking epic and atmospheric soundscapes primarily associated with fantasy and medieval settings through the usage of synthesizers, keyboards, lo-fi production, and drum machines.@@@通过使用合成器、键盘、低保真制作和鼓机，专注于营造主要与奇幻和中世纪背景相关的史诗感和氛围感音景。

Electro Hop@@电子嘻哈@@@Emerged in the mid 2000s featuring upbeat rap verses and catchy hooks over four-on-the-floor Electropop and  Electro House-inspired production.@@@兴起于21世纪00年代中期，以四拍为底的电子流行乐和受电子浩室启发的制作上，搭配轻快的说唱段落和朗朗上口的副歌。
Electro-Industrial@@电子工业@@@Formed in the late 1980s and early 1990s; grew out of a combination of EBM and Industrial.@@@形成于20世纪80年代末至90年代初；由EBM和工业音乐结合发展而来。
Electroacoustic@@电子原声@@@Manipulation and processing of sound sources to create atmospheric pieces.@@@通过对声源进行操控和处理来创作氛围感乐曲。

Electronic Dance Music@@电子舞曲@@@Broad category for subgenres mainly derived from Disco, featuring Electronic sounds, synthesizers, drum machines and varying BPM ranges.@@@主要衍生自迪斯科的子流派的广泛类别，以电子音效、合成器、鼓机和变化的BPM范围为特征。
Electropop@@电子流行@@@Dense, layered, and compressed production, usually coupled with a distinct fuzzy and "warm" low-frequency synthesizer style.@@@密集、分层且压缩的制作，通常搭配一种独特的模糊且“温暖”的低频合成器风格。
Epic Collage@@史诗拼贴@@@Cinematic, dense digital collage; frequently utilizes a Deconstructed Club sonic palette and decontextualized novel samples.@@@电影化、密集的数字拼贴；频繁运用解构俱乐部的声音调色板和脱离语境的新奇采样。

Flashcore@@闪核@@@Fast-paced short sounds, complex structures with erratic progression, and often dense and atmospheric abstract sound design, featuring recontextualized elements from IDM, Speedcore, Industrial Hardcore, and Breakcore.@@@快节奏的短促音效、具有不规则进展的复杂结构，以及常为密集且氛围化的抽象声音设计，融合了来自智能舞曲、速核、工业硬核和碎核的再语境化元素。
Folktronica@@电子民谣@@@Combines sound design techniques from IDM and Glitch with acoustic instrumentation inspired by Contemporary Folk.@@@将智能舞曲和故障音乐的声音设计技巧与受当代民谣启发的原声乐器演奏相结合。
Funktronica@@电子放克@@@Originated in eastern North America around the turn of the 21st century, combining trends of Synth Funk and Italo-Disco.@@@起源于21世纪之交的北美东部地区，融合了合成器放克和意大利迪斯科的趋势。
Glitch@@故障电子@@@Uses abstract sound design and malfunctioning electronics—CD skips, data corruption, and hardware noise—as main compositional elements.@@@以抽象的声音设计和故障电子音效——如CD跳针、数据损坏和硬件噪音——作为主要创作元素。
Glitch Hop@@故障嘻哈@@@Incorporation of IDM-derived production into drum patterns inspired by Hip Hop beats.@@@将IDM风格的制作手法融入受嘻哈节拍启发的鼓点模式中。

Graphical Sound@@图形声音@@@Compositional technique in which markings are manually applied to a medium, then processed by a machine to produce synthetic sound from the markings in real time.@@@一种作曲技术，即手动在介质上做标记，然后通过机器处理，实时从这些标记中生成合成声音。

HexD@@HexD@@@Heavily bit-crushed vocals, sometimes sped-up and pitched-up, and highly compressed and distorted production, typically made from existing material of other genres.@@@大量使用比特压缩的人声，有时会加速和升调，以及高度压缩和失真的制作，通常取材于其他流派的现有素材。

Horror Synth@@恐怖合成器@@@Combines eerie and haunting synth pads and sharp, abrasive sounds to create atmospheres that accentuated the dark, unnerving, violent, or ghostly themes of horror films.@@@结合诡异惊悚的合成器铺垫与尖锐刺耳的音效，营造出强化恐怖电影中黑暗、不安、暴力或幽灵般主题的氛围。

Hyperpop@@超流行@@@Developed and took inspiration from Bubblegum Bass and popular 2000s-2010s Electropop, characterized by eclectic, unconventional, mechanical-sounding and dense production; and by manipulated, usually auto-tuned vocals.@@@从泡泡糖贝斯和2000-2010年代流行的电子流行乐中发展并汲取灵感，其特点是折衷、非传统、机械感且密集的制作，以及经过处理（通常使用自动调音）的人声。

IDM@@智能舞曲@@@Emerged through the development of Electronic Dance Music idioms into a less club-oriented and more experimental direction, often incorporating unconventional sound design and complex rhythms.@@@通过将电子舞音乐语汇向更少俱乐部导向、更具实验性的方向发展而出现，常融入非传统的声音设计和复杂的节奏。
Illbient@@病态氛围@@@Combines Ambient soundscapes with Hip Hop drum patterns, breaks, and samples through the busy effect layering of Dub.@@@通过Dub音乐繁复的效果分层，将氛围音乐的音景与嘻哈鼓点模式、碎拍和采样相结合。

Indietronica@@独立电子@@@Leftfield or DIY approach characterised by simpler Pop-informed melodies, often incorporating vocals or live instrumentation alongside Electronic production.@@@一种左翼或DIY风格，其特点是受流行乐影响的较简单的旋律，常在电子制作中融入人声或现场乐器演奏。
Latin Electronic@@拉丁电子@@@Combines Electronic with Latin American influences; started at the end of the 1990s.@@@将电子音乐与拉丁美洲影响相结合；始于1990年代末。
Livetronica@@现场电子@@@Incorporates Electronic instrumentation into live Rock jamming, drawing on a wide variety of Electronic Dance Music and Downtempo styles.@@@将电子乐器融入现场摇滚即兴演奏，借鉴了多种电子舞曲和缓拍音乐风格。
Maloya électronique@@电子马洛亚@@@Combines the 6/8 ternary rhythm with Electronic production, using computer music, synthesizers and drum machines.@@@将6/8拍的三拍子节奏与电子制作相结合，使用电脑音乐、合成器和鼓机。
Micromontage@@微蒙太奇@@@Arrangement of microsounds on an incredibly small time scale.@@@在极其微小的时间尺度上对微声音进行编排。

Minimal Wave@@极简浪潮@@@Retrospective term for more Electronic and minimal forms of New Wave, Post-Punk, Synthpop, and related styles, tending towards a more sparse and lo-fi sound.@@@对新浪潮、后朋克、合成器流行及相关风格中更电子化和极简形式的回顾性术语，倾向于更稀疏和低保真的声音。
Moogsploitation@@穆格剥削@@@Moog synthesizer-led music often associated with a wave of Novelty records in the 1960s and 1970s.@@@以穆格合成器为主导的音乐，常与1960和1970年代的一波新奇唱片浪潮相关联。
Nightcore@@夜核@@@Based around the technique of speeding and pitching up pre-existing songs.@@@基于对现有歌曲进行加速和升调的技术。
Nu Jazz@@新爵士@@@Electronic styles which feature aesthetics and occasionally instrumentation inspired by Jazz.@@@电子风格，其美学特征及偶尔的器乐灵感源自爵士乐。
Power Electronics@@强力电子@@@Waves of feedback, screeching analog synthesizers, and harsh, screamed vocals.@@@反馈声浪、刺耳的模拟合成器与尖锐嘶吼的人声。
Power Noise@@强力噪音@@@Combines looped samples of Noise with Electronic rhythms.@@@将循环采样的噪音与电子节奏相结合。

Progressive Electronic@@前卫电子@@@Synthesizer-driven genre focusing on developing, often longform compositions and drawing inspiration from various sources such as Progressive Rock, Western Classical Music, and Ambient.@@@以合成器驱动的流派，专注于发展（通常是长篇）作品，并从多种来源汲取灵感，如前卫摇滚、西方古典音乐和环境音乐。
Space Ambient@@太空氛围@@@Flowing and relaxing synthesizer-based music derived from Progressive Electronic, often oriented around imagery of outer space.@@@源自前卫电子的流畅、放松的合成器音乐，常以外太空意象为核心。
Synth Punk@@合成器朋克@@@Appropriates the harsh elements of Punk Rock but replaces the predominance of guitars with synthesizers and drum machines.@@@借鉴了朋克摇滚的粗粝元素，但用合成器和鼓机取代了吉他的主导地位。
Synthpop@@合成器流行@@@Led by melodic synthesizer sounds, often with reverberating drum machine patterns; commonly associated with the 1980s.@@@以旋律性的合成器音色为主导，常伴有回响的鼓机节奏；通常与20世纪80年代联系在一起。

Synthwave@@合成器浪潮@@@Rooted in 1980s synth music, characterized by large, throbbing "retro" synths and visual elements, often features upbeat percussion and/or epic, cinematic atmospheres.@@@植根于20世纪80年代的合成器音乐，以庞大、脉动的“复古”合成器音色和视觉元素为特征，常伴有欢快的打击乐和/或史诗般的电影氛围。
Tecnobrega@@科技布雷加@@@Fast and electronic-based style that evolved from Brega calypso, particularly associated with peripheral areas of the city of Belém.@@@源自布雷加卡利普索的快速电子风格，尤其与贝伦市周边地区相关。
Vapor@@蒸汽波@@@Meta-genre beginning with the rise of Vaporwave and the aesthetics that it spawned.@@@始于蒸汽波的兴起及其衍生的美学风格的元流派。

Wave@@浪潮@@@Melancholic, atmospheric, melodic beats, defined by its Trap and Cloud Rap influence, ethereal and cold production style, and abundant use of sub bass.@@@忧郁、氛围化、旋律性的节拍，受陷阱音乐和云说唱影响，具有空灵、冰冷的制作风格，并大量使用低音贝斯。

Witch House@@女巫浩室@@@Nocturnal, occult-themed/horror-inspired, and murky beats with altered, slowed down and heavily processed samples laid on top of slow Hip Hop-derived drum rhythms and droning, dense synthesizers.@@@具有夜间、神秘/恐怖主题的阴暗节拍，将经过改变、降速和重度处理的采样置于缓慢的嘻哈鼓点节奏以及持续、厚重的合成器音效之上。

Pop_Misc@@流行音乐_其它@@@Umbrella of popular styles closely tied to mass production and mass marketing, focusing on catchiness and accessibility through melody, rhythm, lyrics, and hooks.@@@与大规模生产和营销紧密相连的流行风格总称，注重通过旋律、节奏、歌词和记忆点实现朗朗上口和易于接受。
Adult Contemporary@@成人当代@@@Slow to midtempo with a light and soothing tone, emerged as a successful radio format in the late 1970s and early 1980s.@@@慢速到中速，带有轻松舒缓的基调，于20世纪70年代末80年代初作为一种成功的电台音乐格式出现。
Afrobeats@@非洲节拍@@@Originated in West Africa during the early 2010s and includes elements from Pop Rap, Contemporary R&B, and Dancehall.@@@起源于2010年代初的西非，融合了流行说唱、当代节奏布鲁斯和舞厅音乐元素。

Alt-Pop@@另类流行@@@Emerged in the late 2000s and early 2010s, combines chart Pop conventions with alternative/indie genre sensibilities sometimes on a more minimal and contemplative atmosphere, often extracted from Alternative R&B.@@@兴起于2000年代末至2010年代初，将流行榜单的惯例与另类/独立音乐的风格相结合，有时营造出更简约、沉思的氛围，常从另类节奏布鲁斯中汲取灵感。

Arabic Pop@@阿拉伯流行@@@Synthesis of Arabic Music with Western Pop melodies, almost always sung in Arabic and typically performed with Arabic instruments, alongside some Western instruments like the guitar.@@@阿拉伯音乐与西方流行旋律的融合，几乎总是用阿拉伯语演唱，通常使用阿拉伯乐器，并辅以吉他等一些西方乐器。

Art Pop@@艺术流行@@@Inventive, idiosyncratic, or artistically ambitious, often experimenting with unconventional musical elements and conceptual approaches while retaining accessibility.@@@富有创意、独具特色或艺术上雄心勃勃，常尝试非传统的音乐元素和概念性手法，同时保持可听性。
Balkan Pop-Folk@@巴尔干流行民谣@@@Balkan popular music which blends Pop, Folk, and ethnic music.@@@融合了流行、民谣和民族音乐的巴尔干流行音乐。

Baroque Pop@@巴洛克流行@@@Incorporates elements of Western Classical Music, commonly featuring harpsichord or strings as well as the rhythmic flow and counterpoint of Baroque Music.@@@融入了西方古典音乐元素，通常以羽管键琴或弦乐为特色，并带有巴洛克音乐的节奏流动与对位法。

Bitpop@@位元流行@@@Fuses Bit Music with additional synths, beats, guitars and modern production values, emphasizing highly catchy melodies and relatively fast tempos.@@@将比特音乐与额外的合成器、节拍、吉他及现代制作水准相融合，强调高度抓耳的旋律和相对较快的节奏。

Blue-Eyed Soul@@蓝眼灵魂乐@@@Usually refers to a Pop or Pop Rock song that contains strong Gospel-like elements, primarily popularized by white American and British singers in the 1960s through the 1980s.@@@通常指包含强烈福音音乐元素的流行或流行摇滚歌曲，主要由20世纪60年代至80年代的美国和英国白人歌手推广开来。
Boy Band@@男孩乐队@@@Male vocal groups often put together by talent managers or record producers.@@@通常由星探或唱片制作人组建的男性声乐团体。

Brega calypso@@布雷加卡里普索@@@Romantic vocals and lyrics, bubbly and sensual melodies and rhythms, horn sections and lush Caribbean-like guitar riffs over fast tempos.@@@浪漫的演唱和歌词，活泼而感性的旋律与节奏，铜管乐段以及华丽如加勒比海风情的吉他连复段，配以快节奏。

Brill Building@@布里尔大厦音乐@@@Elaborate, polished, and typically melodramatic composition and arrangement associated with the songwriting teams at the Brill office building during the late 1950s and early 1960s.@@@精致、华丽且通常带有情节剧色彩的作品与编曲，与20世纪50年代末至60年代初布里尔大厦的歌曲创作团队相关联。
Bubblegum@@泡泡糖流行@@@Upbeat, catchy, simple, and targeted at children and young teens; especially popular in the 1960s and 1970s.@@@节奏欢快、朗朗上口、简单，面向儿童和青少年；在20世纪60年代和70年代尤为流行。
Cambodian Pop@@柬埔寨流行@@@Popular music produced in Cambodia or by Khmer people.@@@在柬埔寨制作或由高棉人创作的流行音乐。

Canción melódica@@旋律歌曲@@@Sentimental vocal music sung in Spanish with a focus on the chorus, originally developed in the first half of the 20th century from a variety of traditional Hispanic genres.@@@用西班牙语演唱的感伤声乐，注重副歌部分，最初于20世纪上半叶从多种传统西班牙语流派发展而来。

City Pop@@城市流行@@@Wave of Japanese Pop intended to reflect urban life during the period of the country's booming economy in the 1970s and 1980s, featuring a contemporary Western sound and lush arrangements.@@@日本流行音乐浪潮，旨在反映该国20世纪70至80年代经济繁荣时期的都市生活，融合当代西方音乐风格与丰富的编曲。
Classical Crossover@@古典跨界@@@Takes specific Western Classical Music conventions and applies them into a straightfoward Pop context for mainstream appeal.@@@将特定的西方古典音乐惯例应用于直白的流行音乐语境中，以吸引主流听众。
Country Pop@@乡村流行@@@Combination of Country with Pop to appeal to a larger audience.@@@乡村音乐与流行音乐的结合，旨在吸引更广泛的受众。
Dance-Pop@@流行舞曲@@@Simple, yet catchy melodies and throbbing beats inspired initially by Disco and later various House styles.@@@简单却朗朗上口的旋律和强劲的节拍，最初受迪斯科启发，后来融合了各种浩室音乐风格。

Dangdut@@当杜特@@@Indonesian popular music distinguished by a rhythmic blend of Indian, Arabic, Western, Malay, Sundanese, and Javanese influences within Indonesian Music, characterized by a unique rhythmic pattern from kendang.@@@印度尼西亚流行音乐，其特点是在印尼音乐中融合了印度、阿拉伯、西方、马来、巽他及爪哇音乐的节奏影响，并以甘当鼓独特的节奏模式为标志。

Dansbandsmusik@@舞队音乐@@@Nordic Pop style originating from 1960s Pop Rock, incorporating Swing, Schlager, Nordic Old Time Dance Music and Country influences.@@@源自20世纪60年代流行摇滚的北欧流行风格，融合了摇摆乐、施拉格音乐、北欧旧时舞曲和乡村音乐的影响。
Dansktop@@丹麦流行@@@Danish equivalent to Dansbandsmusik with Schlager and Country influences and Contemporary Folk and Pop elements.@@@丹麦的舞曲乐队音乐，受施拉格音乐和乡村音乐影响，并包含当代民谣和流行音乐元素。

Electro Hop@@电子嘻哈@@@Emerged in the mid 2000s featuring upbeat rap verses and catchy hooks over four-on-the-floor Electropop and  Electro House-inspired production.@@@兴起于21世纪00年代中期，特点是在四拍为底的电子流行乐和受电子浩室启发的制作上，搭配欢快的饶舌段落和抓耳的副歌。
Electropop@@电子流行@@@Dense, layered, and compressed production, usually coupled with a distinct fuzzy and "warm" low-frequency synthesizer style.@@@制作密集、层次丰富且压缩感强，通常伴有独特模糊且"温暖"的低频合成器风格。

Europop@@欧洲流行@@@Emerged throughout mainland Europe in the early 1970s, referring to slick, highly commercial Pop songs that emphasize sugary melodies and light, bouncy instrumentation.@@@20世纪70年代初在欧洲大陆兴起，指代流畅、高度商业化的流行歌曲，强调甜美的旋律和轻快、富有弹性的器乐编排。
Flamenco Pop@@弗拉明戈流行@@@Incorporates Flamenco sub-styles into a more commercial Pop form; particularly through the vocal style and rhythms.@@@将弗拉门戈的子风格融入更商业化的流行音乐形式；尤其通过演唱风格和节奏体现。

Folk Pop@@民谣流行@@@Blends Contemporary Folk with a Pop sensibility, most popular in the late 1960s and early 1970s and featuring influence from the contemporary Folk Rock style.@@@将当代民谣与流行乐感融合，在20世纪60年代末至70年代初最为流行，并受到当时民谣摇滚风格的影响。
French Pop@@法国流行@@@Pop originating from France.@@@源自法国的流行音乐。
Girl Group@@女孩团体@@@Combines elements of Rhythm & Blues, Rock & Roll, and Traditional Pop; typically performed by trios or quartets of female vocalists.@@@融合节奏布鲁斯、摇滚乐和传统流行音乐元素；通常由三人或四人女子演唱组合表演。
Hmong Pop@@苗族流行@@@Popular music of the Hmong ethnicity, with roots in the Hmong Folk Music and influenced by Western and East Asian forms of Pop music.@@@苗族流行音乐，根植于苗族民间音乐，并受西方和东亚流行音乐形式的影响。

Hyperpop@@超流行@@@Developed and took inspiration from Bubblegum Bass and popular 2000s-2010s Electropop, characterized by eclectic, unconventional, mechanical-sounding and dense production; and by manipulated, usually auto-tuned vocals.@@@发展并借鉴了泡泡糖贝斯和2000-2010年代流行的电子流行乐，特点是折衷、非传统、机械感和密集的制作；以及经过处理、通常使用自动调音的人声。

Indian Pop@@印度流行@@@Commonly based out of India, Pakistan and Bangladesh and almost always sung in Hindi, Urdu or Bengali, implements South Asian Music in context of American and European Pop and Rock music.@@@通常源于印度、巴基斯坦和孟加拉国，几乎总是用印地语、乌尔都语或孟加拉语演唱，将南亚音乐元素融入美国和欧洲的流行与摇滚音乐语境中。
Indie Pop@@独立流行@@@Back-to-basics Rock approach combining a less mainstream and DIY ethos with melodic, lighthearted and straightforward songwriting.@@@回归基础的摇滚方式，将不那么主流和DIY的精神与旋律化、轻松且直接的歌曲创作相结合。
Irish Showband@@爱尔兰演出秀乐队@@@Phenomenon lasting from the mid-1950s until the early 1980s, often performed a mixture of currently popular music and skits.@@@从20世纪50年代中期持续到80年代初的现象，通常混合表演当时流行的音乐和滑稽短剧。
Italo Pop@@意大利流行@@@Romantic style developed in Italy in the late 1950s including various influences such as Chanson and Schlager.@@@20世纪50年代末在意大利发展起来的浪漫风格，融合了香颂和施拉格等多种影响。

J-Pop@@日本流行@@@Commercial Pop of Japan after the advent of the Heisei era (1989), characterized by modernized production, a more eclectic sound palette, and a more westernized vocal style.@@@日本平成时代（1989年）开始后的商业流行乐，特点是现代化的制作、更折衷的声音调色板以及更西化的演唱风格。
Jazz Pop@@爵士流行@@@Pop that incorporates elements from Jazz.@@@融合了爵士乐元素的流行音乐。
K-Pop@@韩国流行@@@Contemporary style from South Korea, based on multiple mainstream influences such as Contemporary R&B, Dance-Pop, and Hip Hop.@@@来自韩国的当代风格，基于当代R&B、流行舞曲和嘻哈等多种主流影响。
Kayōkyoku@@歌谣曲@@@Japanese popular music prominent during the later period of the Shōwa era (early 1950s to late 1980s).@@@日本昭和时代后期（20世纪50年代初至80年代末）盛行的流行音乐。
Korean Ballad@@韩国抒情@@@South Korean Pop from the 1980s to the early 90s taking influence from western Blues, Synthpop, Soul and Contemporary Folk.@@@20世纪80年代至90年代初的韩国流行乐，受到西方布鲁斯、合成器流行、灵魂乐和当代民谣的影响。
Latin Pop@@拉丁流行@@@Incorporates elements and tropes from Hispanic and Latin American forms of music into a commercial Pop context.@@@将西班牙裔和拉丁美洲音乐形式的元素与主题融入商业流行乐语境中。
Lokal musik@@地方音乐@@@Created by local, rural artists from Papua New Guinea that incorporate some elements of Papuan Folk Music into Pop.@@@由巴布亚新几内亚本土乡村艺术家创作，将巴布亚民间音乐的一些元素融入流行乐中。
Mulatós@@穆拉托斯@@@Upbeat, danceable Hungarian Pop that takes strong influence from Hungarian Folk Music and Romani Folk Music.@@@欢快、适合跳舞的匈牙利流行乐，深受匈牙利民间音乐和罗姆人（吉普赛）民间音乐的影响。

Nederpop@@荷兰流行@@@Dutch language Pop which originally arose in the 1970s to describe the style of the 1950s and 1960s, but would also later be used for 1980s artists who incorporated elements of Ska and Reggae.@@@荷兰语流行乐，最初在20世纪70年代出现，用以描述50年代和60年代的风格，但后来也用于指代那些融合了斯卡和雷鬼元素的80年代艺术家。
New Music@@新音乐@@@Developed when Japanese Singer-Songwriters incorporated elements of Contemporary Folk and Pop Rock with Kayōkyoku songwriting.@@@日本创作歌手将当代民谣和流行摇滚元素融入歌谣曲创作中发展而成。

OPM@@原创菲律宾音乐@@@Filipino ballads prominent in the 1970s to early 1980s, combining the sentimentality, lush arrangements and vocal techniques of Kundiman with the instrumentations and rhythms of Manila Sound and Soft Rock.@@@盛行于1970年代至1980年代初的菲律宾民谣，融合了昆迪曼的感伤情调、华丽编曲和演唱技巧，以及马尼拉之声和软摇滚的乐器编排与节奏。
Orthodox Pop@@正教流行@@@Combines Orthodox Jewish themes and prayer with Pop songwriting, usually sung in Hebrew.@@@将正统犹太教主题和祈祷文与流行歌曲创作相结合，通常以希伯来语演唱。

P-Pop@@菲律宾流行@@@Commercialized style from the Philippines, which served as the successor of Manila Sound and OPM, featuring various contemporary Western influences and primarily sung in Tagalog and English.@@@菲律宾的商业化风格，作为马尼拉之声和菲律宾原创流行音乐的继承者，融合了多种当代西方音乐影响，主要使用他加禄语和英语演唱。
Persian Pop@@波斯流行@@@Hybrid of Iranian and Persian-language popular music with Western instruments such as the guitar, drum kit, and synthesizer.@@@伊朗及波斯语流行音乐与吉他、架子鼓、合成器等西方乐器的融合。

Pop Batak@@巴塔克流行@@@Combines Western genres of music and instrumentation with a typical Batak flair, like lyrics about Batak life and the use of some traditional Batak instruments.@@@将西方音乐流派和乐器与典型的巴塔克风格相结合，例如歌词描绘巴塔克人的生活，并使用一些传统的巴塔克乐器。
Pop Ghazal@@流行加扎勒@@@Employs traditional Ghazal's verse, meter, and poetic inclinations in a pop form.@@@以流行音乐形式运用传统加扎勒诗的韵律、格律和诗意倾向。
Pop Minang@@米南流行@@@Combines Minangkabau traditions with Pop structures and instrumentation.@@@将米南加保传统与流行音乐结构和乐器编排相结合。
Pop Ra?@@流行拉伊@@@Fuses melodic sensibilities into the traditional Ra? structure, with increased utilization of synthesizers, guitars, and drum machines.@@@将旋律感融入传统的拉格结构，并更多地使用合成器、吉他和鼓机。
Pop Reggae@@流行雷鬼@@@Light, commercial form of Reggae designed to crossover and break through to a more mainstream Pop audience.@@@雷鬼乐的轻快商业化形式，旨在跨界吸引更主流的流行音乐听众。
Pop Rock@@流行摇滚@@@Fuses conventional verse-chorus song structure and especially melodic songwriting with usage of guitars, drums, and propulsive rhythms.@@@融合了传统的副歌-主歌歌曲结构，尤其是旋律化的歌曲创作，并运用吉他、鼓和强劲的节奏。
Pop Soul@@流行灵魂@@@Fusion of Pop and Soul; originated in the 1960s as an attempt to give soul crossover success.@@@流行乐与灵魂乐的融合；起源于1960年代，旨在让灵魂乐获得跨界成功。
Pop Sunda@@巽他流行@@@Mixes Sundanese traditions with Pop structures and instrumentation.@@@将巽他传统与流行音乐结构和乐器编排相融合。

Progressive Pop@@前卫流行@@@Milder counterpart to Progressive Rock, emerging in the late 1960s and introducing complex songwriting and arrangements into a Pop context.@@@前卫摇滚的温和变体，于1960年代末出现，将复杂的歌曲创作和编曲引入流行音乐语境。
Psychedelic Pop@@迷幻流行@@@Combines unconventional instrumentation with studio effects to create a psychedelic atmosphere within melodic Pop music.@@@将非常规乐器与录音室效果结合，在旋律性流行音乐中营造迷幻氛围。

Rabiz@@拉比兹@@@Elements of Armenian folk in its instrumentation, oftentimes incorporating influences from Arabic and other West Asian forms on a background of synthesizer arrangements.@@@乐器编排中融入亚美尼亚民间音乐元素，常在合成器编曲的背景上，结合阿拉伯及其他西亚音乐形式的影响。
Rigsar@@里格萨尔@@@Dominant form of popular music in Bhutan which broke traditions to move towards a more Western and Indian approach.@@@不丹的主流流行音乐形式，它打破传统，转向更西方化和印度化的风格。
Rumba catalana@@加泰罗尼亚伦巴@@@Cheerful Catalan genre that takes influences from Rumba cubana, Andalusian Rumba flamenca, and Pop.@@@一种欢快的加泰罗尼亚音乐类型，融合了古巴伦巴、安达卢西亚弗拉门戈伦巴和流行音乐的影响。
Russian Chanson@@俄罗斯香颂@@@Divided into lyrical and criminal chanson, it is best described as narrative-driven popular music inspired by crime and prison.@@@分为抒情香颂和犯罪香颂，最好将其描述为受犯罪和监狱启发的叙事驱动型流行音乐。
Schlager@@施拉格@@@Light, sentimental, melodic pop music common mainly in northern and central Europe.@@@主要在北部和中欧地区常见的轻快、感伤、旋律优美的流行音乐。
Sertanejo romantico@@浪漫塞塔内霍@@@Electric instrumentation and romantic lyrics, made popular nationwide during the mid-to-late 1980s.@@@电声乐器和浪漫歌词，在20世纪80年代中后期在全国范围内流行起来。
Sertanejo universitário@@大学塞塔内霍@@@Modernized style combining rural themes and acoustic instrumentation with commercial production and Electronic instrumentation.@@@现代化的风格，将乡村主题和原声乐器与商业制作和电子乐器相结合。

Sophisti-Pop@@精致流行@@@Incorporates influences from Jazz, classic R&B and Soul along with a slick, polished production style while retaining elements from its New Wave roots.@@@融合了爵士乐、经典节奏布鲁斯和灵魂乐的影响，以及流畅、精致的制作风格，同时保留了其新浪潮根源的元素。

Soviet Estrada@@苏联舞台乐@@@Emerged in the USSR during the early 1930s, and encompassed the dominating popular music style composed by government-approved songwriters and the primary repertoire of professionally trained performers.@@@20世纪30年代初在苏联出现，涵盖了由政府批准的歌曲作者创作的主流流行音乐风格，以及受过专业训练的表演者的主要曲目。
Sunshine Pop@@阳光流行@@@Emerged in the mid-1960s, characterized by a cheerful or wistful sound, light, lush production, and prominent vocal harmonies.@@@出现于20世纪60年代中期，特点是欢快或忧郁的声音、轻盈丰富的制作以及突出的和声。
Synthpop@@合成器流行@@@Led by melodic synthesizer sounds, often with reverberating drum machine patterns; commonly associated with the 1980s.@@@以旋律性的合成器音色为主导，通常伴有混响的鼓机节奏；通常与20世纪80年代相关联。
Teen Pop@@青少年流行@@@Oriented to teen audiences and almost always sung by teens.@@@面向青少年听众，几乎总是由青少年演唱。

Toytown Pop@@玩具城流行@@@Niche offshoot of British Psychedelic Pop most popular in the late 1960s and early 70s; typically featuring strong influence from Music Hall and Novelty, as well as the culture of Victorian and Edwardian Britain, and characterised by a whimsical, childlike atmosphere.@@@英国迷幻流行音乐的一个小众分支，在20世纪60年代末和70年代初最为流行；通常深受音乐厅和滑稽歌曲以及维多利亚和爱德华时代英国文化的影响，特点是充满异想天开、童趣的氛围。

Traditional Pop@@传统流行@@@Predominant form of Western commercial?popular music?in the pre-Rock & Roll?era, particularly in the USA and starting with Tin Pan Alley; typically vocal-oriented with an orchestral arrangement.@@@前摇滚乐时代西方商业流行音乐的主要形式，尤其在美国，始于叮砰巷时期；通常以人声为主，配以管弦乐编曲。
Turkish Pop@@土耳其流行@@@Started in the late 1950s, when Turkish artists started to write their own lyrics to Western popular tunes.@@@始于20世纪50年代末，当时土耳其艺术家开始为西方流行曲调填上自己的歌词。

Vocal Trance@@人声迷幻@@@Melodic, polished, Pop-like, and vocal-focused with around 120-145 BPM and frequent overlap with Progressive Trance, Uplifting Trance, and Euro Trance.@@@旋律优美、制作精良、类似流行音乐、以人声为主，BPM大约在120-145之间，常与渐进式迷幻、振奋迷幻和欧陆迷幻音乐重叠。
Yé-yé@@耶耶@@@Upbeat, trendy Pop that emerged in Southern Europe in the early 1960s, focusing on image and risqué lyrics.@@@20世纪60年代初在南欧出现的欢快、时髦的流行音乐，注重形象和大胆的歌词。

Sacred Singing Circle@@神圣合唱圈@@@Phenomenon that originally began in hippie gatherings, notably beginning with the counter-culture Rainbow Gatherings of the 1970s, features loose musical jams with varied and spontaneous performances.@@@最初始于嬉皮士聚会，特别是20世纪70年代反主流文化的"彩虹聚会"，以松散的音乐即兴演奏和多样、自发的表演为特色。
Gospel_Misc@@福音音乐_其它@@@Arising from vocal Christian tradition often featuring emotive performances, layered accompaniments, and themes of faith and salvation.@@@源于基督教声乐传统，常以情感充沛的表演、层次丰富的伴奏以及信仰与救赎为主题。
Country Gospel@@乡村福音@@@Incorporates the harmonies and Christian themes of Gospel.@@@融合了福音音乐的和声与基督教主题。
Jubilee@@禧年@@@Rooted in African-American culture, characterized by complex rhythms and call-and-response vocals.@@@根植于非裔美国人文化，以复杂的节奏和呼应式演唱为特点。
Praise Break@@赞美休息@@@High-energy, improvisational "break" for worshiping God in Black churches.@@@在黑人教堂中用于敬拜上帝的高能量、即兴的"突破"音乐。
Sacred Steel@@神圣钢铁@@@Focused on instrumentals and played on the steel guitar.@@@以器乐演奏为主，使用钢棒吉他演奏。

Southern Gospel@@南方福音@@@Initially sung A cappella by a Vocal Group quartet, sometimes with a pianist or guitar as accompaniment, with lyrics and messages primarily revolving around the teachings found within Christianity.@@@最初由声乐四重唱组合无伴奏演唱，有时以钢琴或吉他伴奏，歌词和主旨主要围绕基督教教义展开。

Traditional Black Gospel@@传统黑人福音@@@Born out of Spirituals in the early 20th century with influence from Southern Gospel and Blues, often featuring passionate or energetic vocal performances, motifs of salvation, and testaments of hardship.@@@20世纪初从灵歌中诞生，受南方福音音乐和布鲁斯影响，常以充满激情或活力的演唱、救赎主题以及对苦难的见证为特征。
Urban Contemporary Gospel@@都市当代福音@@@Inspired by Gospel music, featuring Christian lyrics adapted to contemporary rhythms and styles.@@@受福音音乐启发，将基督教歌词融入当代节奏和风格中。
Hymns_Misc@@赞美诗_其它@@@Performed and written with the purpose of praise, adoration, and prayer, often addressed to deities or other cultural figures.@@@以赞美、敬仰和祈祷为目的进行表演和创作，通常献给神明或其他文化人物。
Bhajan@@拜赞歌@@@Song-based form of devotional music within Dharmic religions, with lyrical themes having an emphasis on deep adoration for a deity.@@@达摩宗教中基于歌曲形式的虔敬音乐，歌词主题强调对神明的深切敬爱。
Lauda@@劳达@@@Vernacular, devotional sung poetry, composed in Italy in the late Medieval and Renaissance periods.@@@用意大利语演唱的、本土化的虔敬诗歌，创作于中世纪晚期和文艺复兴时期的意大利。
Purísima@@纯真颂@@@Nicaraguan Hymns to the Immaculate Conception of Mary, sung during holiday celebrations and in liturgical or parochial services.@@@尼加拉瓜的《圣母无染原罪赞歌》，在节日庆典以及礼拜或堂区仪式中演唱。
Sacred Harp Singing@@神圣竖琴合唱@@@Predominantly 19th-century English hymns with tunes derived from old folk melodies.@@@主要是19世纪的英国赞美诗，其曲调源自古老的民间旋律。

Mantra_Misc@@真言唱颂_其它@@@Sounds, words or phrases, often repeatedly chanted, used in religions originating in the Indian subcontinent, and spiritual practices influenced by them.@@@声音、词语或短语，常被反复吟诵，用于起源于印度次大陆的宗教以及受其影响的灵修实践。

Tibetan Buddhist Chant@@藏传佛教唱颂@@@Ritual vocal music of the Tibetan Buddhist faith characterized by its low pitch, droning repetitiousness, and frequent use of overtone singing.@@@藏传佛教的仪式声乐，特点是音调低沉、重复吟诵，并频繁使用泛音唱法。
Vedic Chant@@吠陀唱颂@@@Sacred Hindu chants rooted in the ancient Vedas, and primarily recited as mantras, requiring strict precision and diligence.@@@植根于古老吠陀经的印度教神圣诵唱，主要作为咒语念诵，要求极其精确和勤勉。
New Age_Misc@@新世纪音乐_其它@@@Broad genre centered on peaceful, meditative melodies, using a wide variety of instrumentation.@@@以平和、冥想的旋律为核心，使用多种乐器的大类音乐。
Andean New Age@@安第斯新世纪@@@Takes heavy influence from Indigenous Andean Music, emphasizing a peaceful and harmonious atmosphere and often using the flute.@@@深受安第斯土著音乐影响，强调平和和谐的氛围，常使用笛子。
Celtic New Age@@凯尔特新世纪@@@Incorporates influences and performing styles from Celtic Folk Music, emphasizing pastoral imagery and wistful themes.@@@融合凯尔特民间音乐的影响和表演风格，强调田园意象和怀旧主题。

Native American New Age@@美国原住民新世纪@@@Takes heavy influence from Indigenous North American Music, emphasizing a peaceful and harmonious atmosphere and utilizing chants, drumming, and flutes.@@@深受北美土著音乐影响，强调平和和谐的氛围，运用吟唱、鼓声和笛子。
Neoclassical New Age@@新古典新世纪@@@Strongly influenced by and sometimes also based upon early music and the Baroque Music or Classical Period.@@@深受早期音乐、巴洛克音乐或古典时期音乐影响，有时也基于这些音乐。
New Age Kirtan@@新世纪科尔坦@@@American development of Kirtan, fusing it with New Age and Western music.@@@美国对克伊尔坦的发展，将其与新世纪音乐和西方音乐融合。
Tibetan New Age@@西藏新世纪@@@Incorporates perceived elements of Tibetan Music, particularly singing bowls.@@@融入了感知到的西藏音乐元素，特别是颂钵。
Dark Cabaret@@黑暗卡巴莱@@@Dark, moody style incorporating elements of Cabaret in a modern context, with varied instrumentation.@@@一种黑暗、忧郁的风格，在现代语境中融入了卡巴莱元素，配器多样。

Industrial & Noise_Misc@@工业与噪音_其它@@@Umbrella encompassing Noise and Industrial, as well as later derivatives that combine the latter aesthetics with other styles and in more accessible directions which fall under Post-Industrial.@@@一个涵盖噪音音乐和工业音乐的伞状术语，也包括后来将后者的美学与其他风格结合、并走向更易接受方向的衍生流派，这些属于后工业音乐范畴。

Industrial@@工业音乐@@@Emerged out of Punk and avant-garde art movements in the mid-1970s, often characterized by a bleak, mechanical atmosphere, the use of metallic percussion as well as other unconventional instruments, and extensive use of sampling influenced by Musique concrète.@@@起源于20世纪70年代中期的朋克和前卫艺术运动，通常以阴郁、机械的氛围为特征，使用金属打击乐及其他非常规乐器，并受到具象音乐影响而大量使用采样。
Noise@@噪音音乐@@@Strays away from conventional structure, tonality, and composition and consists predominantly of noise.@@@偏离传统的结构、调性和作曲方式，主要由噪音构成。

Post-Industrial@@后工业@@@Umbrella group of genres that combine the dark, mechanical aesthetic of early Industrial music with more accessible genres, such as Rock and Electronic Dance Music.@@@一种融合了早期工业音乐的黑暗机械美学与更易接近的摇滚和电子舞曲等流派的音乐类别。

Metal_Misc@@金属乐_其它@@@Driving and distorted riffs, aggressive drumming, vigorous vocals, and an all-around show of brute force in its early days, since branching into dozens of subgenres.@@@早期以强劲失真的连复段、侵略性的鼓点、有力的演唱和全方位的蛮力展示为特征，后来发展出数十种子流派。
Alternative Metal@@另类金属@@@Mid-tempo style emphasizing melodicism and rhythmic heaviness; branches outside of the stylistic conventions of Metal.@@@中速风格，强调旋律性和节奏沉重感；风格上超越了金属乐的常规界限。
Avant-Garde Metal@@前卫金属@@@Often breaking the bounds of the genre through experimental structures and instrumentation, or by incorporating outside influences.@@@常通过实验性的结构和配器，或融入外部影响来突破流派的界限。

Black Metal@@黑金属@@@Highly distorted, treble-heavy guitars, tremolo-picked riffs, blast beats and double bass drumming, shrieked vocals, and raw, lo-fi production; often focuses on occult, dark imagery and atmosphere.@@@高度失真、高音突出的吉他，颤音拨弦的连复段，爆炸式鼓点和双踩，尖啸式人声，以及原始、低保真的制作；通常关注神秘、黑暗的意象和氛围。

Death Metal@@死金@@@Heavily distorted, down-tuned guitars, palm-muted, tremolo-picked riffs, double-kick and blast beat percussion, chromatic chord progressions, minor keys, abrupt changes in tempo, and guttural vocals.@@@严重失真、降调处理的吉他，手掌闷音、颤音拨弦的连复段，双踩和爆炸式打击乐，半音阶和弦进行，小调，速度的突然变化，以及喉音演唱。
Djent@@Djent@@@Groovy polymeters, syncopated power chord chugs, palm-muted, distorted guitars, and significant focus on production and tone.@@@律动感强的复节拍，切分的强力和弦连复段，手掌闷音、失真的吉他，以及对制作和音色的高度重视。
Doom Metal@@厄运金属@@@Plodding tempos, repeated chords, and thick, distorted, down-tuned guitars in a style focused on foreboding, atmosphere, and tension.@@@缓慢的节奏、重复的和弦，以及厚重、失真、降调处理的吉他，风格侧重于不祥的氛围与紧张感。

Drone Metal@@嗡鸣金属@@@Extremely slow tempos, highly sustained, repetitive guitar notes, and minimalist song structures often omitting a traditional rhythm section and vocals.@@@极慢的节奏，高度持续、重复的吉他音符，以及极简的歌曲结构，通常省略传统的节奏声部和人声。

Folk Metal@@民谣金属@@@Features influences from various types of Traditional Folk Music expressed through melodies and/or traditional instrumentation, notably having a large scene in Europe.@@@融合了各类传统民间音乐的影响，通过旋律和/或传统乐器表现出来，在欧洲尤其拥有庞大的场景。

Gothic Metal@@哥特金属@@@Features heavy, low-mid tempo riffs, somber, gloomy atmosphere accentuated by ethereal keyboards and synthesizer layers, and vocal styles ranging from raspy growls to female operatic singing.@@@以沉重、中慢速的连复段为特色，空灵的键盘和合成器层次增强了阴郁、幽暗的氛围，人声风格从沙哑的咆哮到女高音歌剧唱腔不一而足。

Grindcore@@碾核@@@Brief and intense song structures featuring a plethora of blast beats, down-tuned and tremolo-picked guitars, and abrasive, usually raw production.@@@歌曲结构简短而激烈，包含大量的爆炸式鼓点、降调及颤音拨弦的吉他，以及刺耳、通常未经精细处理的制作效果。
Groove Metal@@律动金属@@@Midtempo riffs influenced by Thrash Metal but with a greater focus on rhythmic syncopation and heaviness over speed.@@@受激流金属影响的中速连复段，但更侧重于节奏切分和沉重感，而非速度。
Heavy Metal@@重金属@@@Heavier outgrowth of Hard Rock featuring greater distortion and intensity along with lesser Blues influences.@@@硬摇滚的加重分支，具有更强的失真和强度，同时减少了布鲁斯的影响。
Industrial Metal@@工业金属@@@Incorporates the abrasive and heavy sound of Industrial into Metal, using elements like synthesizers and drum machines.@@@将工业音乐的刺耳与沉重融入金属乐，使用合成器和鼓机等元素。

Kawaii Metal@@可爱金属@@@Fuses various Metal substyles with J-Pop's upbeat melodicism and song structures, utilizing kawaii lyricism and an idol music aesthetic.@@@将各种金属子风格与日本流行乐的欢快旋律性和歌曲结构相融合，运用可爱的歌词和偶像音乐的美学。

Metalcore@@金属核@@@Drop-tuned guitar riffs, constant double kick drumming with varying tempos and techniques, breakdown sections, and screaming or shouting vocals.@@@降调处理的吉他连复段，持续变化的双踩鼓节奏与技巧，Breakdown段落，以及嘶吼或呐喊式人声。
Neoclassical Metal@@新古典金属@@@Greatly influenced by Western Classical Music in its style of playing and composing.@@@在演奏和作曲风格上深受西方古典音乐的极大影响。
Post-Metal@@后金属@@@Atmospheric and dense metallic riffs, unconventional songwriting, sonic experimentation, and adoption of eclectic influences.@@@氛围化且密集的金属连复段，非常规的歌曲创作，声音实验，以及兼收并蓄的影响元素。
Power Metal@@力量金属@@@Developed from Heavy Metal and Speed Metal, emphasizing speed, vocal melody and harmonized lead guitars.@@@从重金属和速度金属发展而来，强调速度、人声旋律与和声化的主音吉他。

Progressive Metal@@前卫金属@@@Fuses amplified, guitar-driven sound with technical proficiency, virtuosity, complex compositions, innovative arrangements, eclecticism, and additional instrumentation.@@@融合了放大的、以吉他驱动的声音与技术精湛、大师级演奏、复杂的作曲、创新的编曲、折衷主义以及额外的器乐编排。

Sludge Metal@@污泥金属@@@Mixes Doom Metal's slow pacing, down-tuned riffing style and dark atmosphere with Hardcore Punk's aggression, abrasiveness and harsh vocals.@@@混合了厄运金属的缓慢节奏、降调连复段风格和黑暗氛围，以及硬核朋克的侵略性、粗粝感和刺耳人声。
Southern Metal@@南方金属@@@Blues-influenced style that primarily incorporates Sludge Metal and Stoner Metal with metallic Southern Rock riffs.@@@受布鲁斯影响的风格，主要融合了泥浆金属和石人金属，并带有金属化的南方摇滚连复段。
Stenchcore@@恶臭核@@@Heavier, more metallic form of Crust Punk drawing influence from Thrash Metal as well as Death Metal and Heavy Metal.@@@一种更重、更具金属感的硬核朋克，受激流金属、死亡金属和重金属影响。
Stoner Metal@@大麻金属@@@Combines elements of Doom Metal with elements of Psychedelic Rock and Blues Rock to create a melodic yet heavy sound.@@@融合厄运金属元素与迷幻摇滚、蓝调摇滚元素，创造出旋律优美而又沉重的声响。
Symphonic Metal@@交响金属@@@Incorporates elements of symphonic and Orchestral Music.@@@融合了交响乐和管弦乐元素。

Thrash Metal@@敲击金属@@@Blistering tempos, palm-muted, technical riffing influenced by Speed Metal, aggressive drumming utilizing double-kick and skank beats, and diverse vocal styles ranging from harsh to clean techniques.@@@疾速的节奏、受速度金属影响的掌闷音技术性连复段、运用双踩和反拍节奏的激进鼓点，以及从粗粝到清嗓的多样化人声风格。

Trance Metal@@迷幻金属@@@Tempo between 130 and 160 BPM, played with keyboards and metal instrumentation, sung with harsh and aggressive vocals including many clean melodic styles; regularly uses elements of Melodic Death Metal, Symphonic Metal, and Electronic Dance Music.@@@速度在130到160 BPM之间，使用键盘和金属乐器演奏，人声粗粝而富有攻击性，但也包含许多清嗓旋律风格；经常融合旋律死亡金属、交响金属和电子舞曲元素。
Viking Metal@@维京金属@@@Developed out of Black Metal and embodies an epic, Viking-like aesthetic.@@@从黑金属发展而来，并体现出一种史诗般的、维京式的美学。

Psychedelia_Misc@@迷幻音乐_其它@@@Umbrella of styles intended to replicate or enhance the altered state of consciousness brought on by the use of psychedelic drugs, originating in the 1960s.@@@迷幻风格旨在复制或增强由迷幻药物引发的意识改变状态，起源于20世纪60年代。
Neo-Psychedelia@@新迷幻@@@Developments in Psychedelia since the early 1980s, building on the drug-inspired styles developed in the 1960s.@@@自20世纪80年代初以来，在60年代受药物启发的风格基础上发展的迷幻音乐演变。

Psychedelic Folk@@迷幻民谣@@@Acoustic forms that capture an atmosphere that is at turns dream-like, 'spaced-out', whimsical, haunting, mystical, and melancholic.@@@原声音乐形式，捕捉时而梦幻、时而“神游”、异想天开、萦绕心头、神秘且忧郁的氛围。
Psychedelic Pop@@迷幻流行@@@Combines unconventional instrumentation with studio effects to create a psychedelic atmosphere within melodic Pop music.@@@将非常规乐器与录音室效果相结合，在旋律性流行音乐中营造迷幻氛围。
Psychedelic Rock@@迷幻摇滚@@@Rock which emerged in the mid-1960s that often attempts to emulate or enhance the effects of psychedelic drugs.@@@20世纪60年代中期出现的摇滚乐，常试图模仿或增强迷幻药物的效果。
Psychedelic Soul@@迷幻灵魂@@@Soul which incorporates the effects and instrumentation of Psychedelic Rock.@@@融合了迷幻摇滚效果和器乐的灵魂乐。

Psychsploitation@@迷幻剥削@@@Short-lived genre seeking to capitalise on the popularity of Psychedelia in the 1960s, featuring quickly and cheaply recorded material played by session musicians and put out by budget labels.@@@短暂存在的流派，旨在利用20世纪60年代迷幻音乐的流行，特点是录音快速廉价，由录音室乐手演奏，并由廉价厂牌发行。
Stoner Metal@@大麻金属@@@Combines elements of Doom Metal with elements of Psychedelic Rock and Blues Rock to create a melodic yet heavy sound.@@@融合厄运金属元素与迷幻摇滚和布鲁斯摇滚元素，创造出旋律优美却又沉重的音色。
Stoner Rock@@大麻摇滚@@@Psychedelic timbres, raw production, and stylistic influence from Doom Metal and Heavy Psych.@@@迷幻音色、粗粝的制作，以及来自厄运金属和重型迷幻的风格影响。

Tropicália@@热带主义@@@Blends Brazilian popular styles with the influence of Anglo-American Psychedelia; emerged as part of Brazil's late-1960s counterculture.@@@将巴西流行风格与英美迷幻音乐的影响相融合；作为巴西20世纪60年代末反主流文化的一部分出现。
Punk_Misc@@朋克_其它@@@Musical and cultural product of Punk Rock known for its simplistic, brash playstyle and anti-establishment themes.@@@朋克摇滚的音乐与文化产物，以其简单、粗粝的演奏风格和反体制主题而闻名。

Art Punk@@艺术朋克@@@Combines the stripped-down aggression of rudimentary Punk Rock with frequent experimentation ranging from instrumental interplay, noise, dissonance, and/or influences from other genres such as Jazz or Funk.@@@将基础朋克摇滚的极简式攻击性与频繁的实验性相结合，实验范围包括器乐互动、噪音、不和谐音，和/或来自其他流派（如爵士或放克）的影响。
Cowpunk@@牛朋克@@@Mixes together Country Rock, Rockabilly, and Contemporary Folk with the familiar punk sound.@@@将乡村摇滚、山区乡村摇滚和当代民谣与熟悉的朋克声音混合在一起。

Digital Hardcore@@数字硬核@@@High-tempo fusion of Hardcore Punk and Electronic, combining harsh vocals and electric guitars of the former with electronic instruments.@@@硬核朋克与电子音乐的高速融合，结合了前者的刺耳人声和电吉他与电子乐器。
Emo@@情绪硬核@@@Focuses on emotional lyrics, expressive vocals, melodic songwriting, and dynamic song structures.@@@侧重于情感化的歌词、富有表现力的人声、旋律性的歌曲创作以及动态的歌曲结构。
Folk Punk@@民谣朋克@@@Influenced by various forms of Folk, including Traditional Folk Music as well as Contemporary Folk and Folk Rock.@@@受各种民谣形式影响，包括传统民间音乐以及当代民谣和民谣摇滚。

Hardcore [Punk]@@硬核朋克@@@Broad genre which encompasses the first wave of Hardcore Punk beginning in the 1970s, as well as its derivative styles that incorporate diverse musical influences and stray away from the core Punk Rock sound.@@@广义流派，涵盖始于1970年代的第一波硬核朋克，以及融合多样音乐影响并偏离朋克摇滚核心声音的衍生风格。
Mod Revival@@摩斯族复兴@@@Late 1970s and early 1980s revival of Mod fashion and music, featuring influence from the nascent Punk Rock scene.@@@1970年代末至1980年代初的摩登时尚与音乐复兴，受到新兴朋克摇滚场景的影响。

Pigfuck@@猪操@@@Sloppy, abrasive and feedback-laden riffing with vulgar and edgy lyricism, informed by Post-Hardcore's aggression and Post-Punk's rhythm section.@@@松散、粗粝且充满回授的即兴重复段，配以粗俗尖锐的歌词，融合了后硬核的侵略性与后朋克的节奏组特点。
Post-Punk@@后朋克@@@Emerged alongside the initial Punk Rock explosion in the mid-to-late 1970s, putting a greater emphasis on experimentation and atmosphere.@@@与1970年代中后期最初的朋克摇滚爆发同时出现，更侧重于实验性与氛围营造。
Proto-Punk@@原朋克@@@Used to describe what influenced or in some way resembled Punk Rock before that genre's commercial breakthrough in 1976.@@@用于描述在1976年朋克摇滚取得商业突破之前，对其产生影响或在某种程度上与之相似的音乐。
Punk Blues@@朋克蓝调@@@Fusion of the instrumentation and scales of Blues Rock with Punk.@@@布鲁斯摇滚的乐器运用与音阶与朋克的融合。
Punk Poetry@@朋克诗歌@@@Fast and rhythmic vocal delivery of political or satirical themes; frequently backed by Post-Punk or Minimal Wave instrumentation.@@@对政治或讽刺主题进行快速而有节奏的声乐演绎；常以后朋克或极简浪潮的器乐伴奏。

Punk Rock@@朋克摇滚@@@Fast tempos, loud and distorted riffs, simple songs, frequent use of power chords, and shouted vocals, originating in the mid-1970s in response to commercial, sentimental, and complex Rock.@@@快速的节奏、响亮失真的即兴重复段、简单的歌曲结构、频繁使用强力和弦以及呐喊式演唱，起源于1970年代中期，以对抗商业化、感伤化和复杂的摇滚乐。
Synth Punk@@合成器朋克@@@Appropriates the harsh elements of Punk Rock but replaces the predominance of guitars with synthesizers and drum machines.@@@借鉴朋克摇滚的粗粝元素，但用合成器和鼓机取代了吉他的主导地位。

Rock_Misc@@摇滚乐_其它@@@Typically uses a verse-chorus structure with a backbeat rhythm and the electric guitar at the forefront; generally heavier and/or faster than its predecessors.@@@通常采用主歌-副歌结构，带有反拍节奏，并以电吉他为主导；普遍比其前身更重型和/或更快。

AOR@@成人定向摇滚@@@Emerged in the late 1970s and early 1980s as an amalgamation of Hard Rock, Pop Rock, and Progressive Rock; characterized by a rich, layered sound, slick production and a heavy reliance on commercial melodic hooks.@@@于20世纪70年代末80年代初兴起，融合了硬摇滚、流行摇滚和前卫摇滚；特点为丰富、层次分明的音效，精良的制作，以及对商业化旋律钩子的重度依赖。
Acoustic Rock@@原声摇滚@@@Rock with acoustic instrumentation, as opposed to the typical electric instrumentation.@@@使用原声乐器演奏的摇滚乐，与典型的电声乐器配置相对。
Afro-Rock@@非洲摇滚@@@Incorporates West African influences; initially emerged in the early 1970s.@@@融合西非音乐影响；最初兴起于20世纪70年代初。

Alternative Rock@@另类摇滚@@@Performed with a less commercial sensibility, utilizing more eccentric, Punk-influenced sounds, moodier or quirkier lyricism, and sometimes ample amounts of distortion, often paired with Pop-influenced songwriting.@@@以较少的商业敏感性进行演绎，运用更古怪、受朋克影响的声响，更忧郁或奇特的歌词，有时伴有大量失真音效，并常与受流行影响的歌曲创作相结合。

Anatolian Rock@@安纳托利亚摇滚@@@Incorporates elements of Turkish Folk Music, particularly traditional instruments such as the ba?lama and saz alongside traditional scales and melodies.@@@融合土耳其民间音乐元素，特别是传统乐器如巴格拉玛和萨兹，以及传统音阶和旋律。

Art Punk@@艺术朋克@@@Combines the stripped-down aggression of rudimentary Punk Rock with frequent experimentation ranging from instrumental interplay, noise, dissonance, and/or influences from other genres such as Jazz or Funk.@@@将基础朋克摇滚的简约攻击性与频繁的实验性相结合，实验范围包括器乐互动、噪音、不和谐音，和/或来自爵士或放克等其他流派的影响。

Art Rock@@艺术摇滚@@@Attempts to expand the bounds of Rock within conventional structures, often using outside influences or experimentation for conceptual or thematic purpose.@@@试图在传统结构内拓展摇滚乐的边界，常为概念或主题目的而运用外部影响或实验手法。
Bard Rock@@吟游摇滚@@@Mixes Rock music and styles with Avtorskaya pesnya.@@@将摇滚音乐及风格与作者歌曲（Avtorskaya pesnya）相融合。
Blues Rock@@蓝调摇滚@@@Relies on the chords/scales and instrumental improvisation of Blues.@@@依赖于布鲁斯的和弦/音阶和器乐即兴演奏。
British Rhythm & Blues@@英国节奏蓝调@@@A style infusing Rock and R&B that was hugely popular in the United Kingdom between 1963 and 1966.@@@一种融合摇滚与节奏布鲁斯的风格，在1963年至1966年间于英国极为流行。
Candombe beat@@坎东贝节拍@@@Fusion of Uruguayan Candombe with elements of Rock music.@@@乌拉圭坎东贝音乐与摇滚音乐元素的融合。
Christian Rock@@基督教摇滚@@@Incorporates Christian lyrics, with topics such as faith, metaphysics, or biblical characters.@@@融入基督教歌词，主题涉及信仰、形而上学或圣经人物。

Comedy Rock@@喜剧摇滚@@@Roughly defined as a mix of Rock or any of its subgenres with elements of Comedy such as Satire and Musical Parody, as well as concepts of Novelty.@@@大致定义为摇滚或其任何子流派与喜剧元素（如讽刺和音乐模仿）以及新奇概念的混合。
Country Rock@@乡村摇滚@@@Combination of contemporary Rock with more traditional American Country.@@@当代摇滚与更传统的美国乡村音乐的结合。
Cowpunk@@牛朋克@@@Mixes together Country Rock, Rockabilly, and Contemporary Folk with the familiar punk sound.@@@将乡村摇滚、山区乡村摇滚和当代民谣与熟悉的朋克声音融合在一起。

Deutschrock@@德国摇滚@@@Lyrics address German life, culture, and identity wrapped in upbeat songs, anthemic ballads, and a strong focus on the personality of the lead singer.@@@歌词涉及德国生活、文化和身份认同，包裹在欢快的歌曲、颂歌般的民谣中，并强烈聚焦于主唱的个人特质。
Emo@@情绪硬核@@@Focuses on emotional lyrics, expressive vocals, melodic songwriting, and dynamic song structures.@@@注重情感丰富的歌词、富有表现力的演唱、旋律化的歌曲创作以及动态的歌曲结构。

Experimental Rock@@实验摇滚@@@Eschews accessibility and convention, experimenting with song structures, time signatures, rhythm, dissonance, instrumentation, noise, electronics, studio manipulation, and other techniques not traditionally associated with Rock.@@@摒弃通俗性和惯例，在歌曲结构、拍号、节奏、不和谐音、器乐编排、噪音、电子乐、录音室处理以及其他传统上与摇滚无关的技术上进行实验。

Folk Rock@@民谣摇滚@@@Influenced by Folk, particularly in the use of acoustic instrumentation and relatively simple arrangements; has its origins in the American Contemporary Folk movement of the 1960s.@@@受民谣影响，特别是在使用原声乐器和相对简单的编曲方面；起源于1960年代的美国当代民谣运动。
Funk Rock@@放克摇滚@@@Incorporates prominent Funk grooves.@@@融入突出的放克律动。

Garage Rock@@车库摇滚@@@Raw and energetic, generally employing simple, sloppy, and fuzzbox-distorted guitar melodies in addition to frequent shouting or screaming.@@@原始而充满活力，通常采用简单、粗糙且经过法兹盒失真的吉他旋律，并伴有频繁的喊叫或嘶吼。

Glam Rock@@华丽摇滚@@@Incorporates elements of Rock & Roll and Blues Rock into catchy Pop Rock melodies, usually accompanied with a strong sense of theatricality.@@@将摇滚乐和布鲁斯摇滚的元素融入朗朗上口的流行摇滚旋律中，通常伴随着强烈的戏剧感。

Hard Rock@@硬摇滚@@@Originated in the mid-to-late 1960s from Psychedelic Rock and Blues Rock with a heavy sound characterized by distorted guitars and power chords.@@@起源于1960年代中后期，源自迷幻摇滚和布鲁斯摇滚，以其沉重的声音为特征，表现为失真的吉他和强力和弦。

Hardcore [Punk]@@硬核朋克@@@Broad genre which encompasses the first wave of Hardcore Punk beginning in the 1970s, as well as its derivative styles that incorporate diverse musical influences and stray away from the core Punk Rock sound.@@@一个宽泛的流派，涵盖了始于1970年代的第一波硬核朋克，以及其衍生风格，这些风格融合了多样化的音乐影响并偏离了核心的朋克摇滚声音。

Heartland Rock@@中心地带摇滚@@@Borrows elements from Folk Rock, Roots Rock, and 1950s-60s Rock & Roll; often characterized by its conscious, communal lyrical concerns and working-class appeal.@@@借鉴了民谣摇滚、根源摇滚和1950-60年代摇滚乐元素；通常以具有社会意识的集体性歌词主题和工人阶级吸引力为特征。
Industrial Rock@@工业摇滚@@@Incorporates abrasive guitars and synthesizers inspired by Industrial and various Post-Industrial styles.@@@融合了受工业音乐及各类后工业风格启发的粗粝吉他声与合成器音效。

Jam Band@@即兴乐队@@@Arose in late 1960s America, usually performed in a live setting and combines diverse styles under the Rock umbrella, often influenced by genres such as Funk, Jazz, Country and Blues.@@@兴起于1960年代末的美国，通常在现场表演，融合摇滚范畴下的多种风格，常受放克、爵士、乡村和蓝调等流派影响。
Jazz-Rock@@爵士摇滚@@@Strongly influenced by Jazz in structure and/or instrumentation.@@@在结构和/或器乐编排上深受爵士乐影响。

Jersey Shore Sound@@泽西海岸之声@@@Stylistically correlated to Heartland Rock, with cultural origins in coastal New Jersey; characterized by its horn-heavy mixture of 1960s Rock & Roll, Rhythm & Blues, and Doo-Wop.@@@风格上与心之摇滚相关联，文化根源在新泽西州沿海地区；以铜管乐为主导，融合1960年代摇滚乐、节奏蓝调和嘟·喔普音乐为特征。
Latin Rock@@拉丁摇滚@@@Fusion of Rock, often Psychedelic Rock, with Latin American rhythms, especially Afro-Latin genres.@@@摇滚（常为迷幻摇滚）与拉丁美洲节奏的融合，尤指非洲-拉丁风格。
Manguebeat@@曼圭比迪@@@Counterculture fusion of traditional Northeastern Brazilian Music genres with modern Rock and Hip Hop.@@@将巴西东北部传统音乐流派与现代摇滚、嘻哈音乐进行反文化融合。

Math Rock@@数学摇滚@@@Explores complex and unconventional rhythmic patterns through unusual syncopation, stop-start structures, polyrhythms and complex time signatures, angular melodies, and technical instrumental performances.@@@通过非常规切分、起止结构、复节奏与复杂拍号、棱角分明的旋律及技术性器乐演奏，探索复杂且非传统的节奏模式。

Metal@@金属乐@@@Driving and distorted riffs, aggressive drumming, vigorous vocals, and an all-around show of brute force in its early days, since branching into dozens of subgenres.@@@早期以强劲失真的连复段、侵略性的鼓点、充满力量的演唱和全方位的原始力量展现为特征，后衍生出数十种子流派。
Miejski folk@@城市民谣@@@Alternative and Punk music influenced by Folklor miejski and Contemporary Folk.@@@受城市民间传说和当代民谣影响的另类与朋克音乐。

New Wave@@新浪潮@@@Broad term referring to a variety of styles that saw popularity in the wake of the Punk Rock explosion of 1976-77, initially strongly associated with more accessible and stylish offshoots of punk; developed alongside Post-Punk, similarly emphasising freshness and daring.@@@广义术语，指1976-77年朋克摇滚爆发后流行的多种风格，最初与更易接受且时髦的朋克分支紧密相关；与后朋克同期发展，同样强调新颖与大胆。

Noise Rock@@噪音摇滚@@@Explores dissonance and distortion through unconventional playing and guitar effects, commonly augmented by lo-fi production and bursts of feedback.@@@通过非常规演奏技巧和吉他效果探索不和谐音与失真音色，通常辅以低保真制作和反馈噪音爆发。
Pop Rock@@流行摇滚@@@Fuses conventional verse-chorus song structure and especially melodic songwriting with usage of guitars, drums, and propulsive rhythms.@@@融合传统主歌-副歌曲式结构（尤指旋律性创作）与吉他、鼓及推进性节奏的运用。
Post-Punk@@后朋克@@@Emerged alongside the initial Punk Rock explosion in the mid-to-late 1970s, putting a greater emphasis on experimentation and atmosphere.@@@伴随1970年代中后期首次朋克摇滚爆发而兴起，更侧重于实验性与氛围营造。

Post-Rock@@后摇滚@@@Emphasis on timbre, texture, and atmosphere over traditional conventions while often embracing influences from genres not usually associated with Rock.@@@强调音色、质感与氛围而非传统规范，常吸纳通常与摇滚无关的音乐流派影响。
Progressive Rock@@前卫摇滚@@@Complex harmonies, instrumental virtuosity, and usually multi-section song structures featuring a broad range of instrumentation.@@@复杂的和声、精湛的乐器演奏技巧，以及通常采用多段式歌曲结构，配器范围广泛。
Proto-Punk@@原朋克@@@Used to describe what influenced or in some way resembled Punk Rock before that genre's commercial breakthrough in 1976.@@@用于描述在1976年朋克摇滚取得商业突破之前，对其产生影响或在某种程度上与之相似的风格。
Psychedelic Rock@@迷幻摇滚@@@Rock which emerged in the mid-1960s that often attempts to emulate or enhance the effects of psychedelic drugs.@@@兴起于20世纪60年代中期的摇滚乐，常试图模仿或增强迷幻药物的效果。

Pub Rock@@酒吧摇滚@@@Stripped-down style developed in the United Kingdom in the early to mid-1970s, mainly as a reaction to the perceived excess and pretension of Progressive Rock and Glam Rock.@@@20世纪70年代早中期在英国发展起来的一种极简风格，主要是对前卫摇滚和华丽摇滚的过度铺张和矫饰做出的反应。
Punk Blues@@朋克蓝调@@@Fusion of the instrumentation and scales of Blues Rock with Punk.@@@融合了布鲁斯摇滚的乐器和音阶与朋克元素。

Punk Rock@@朋克摇滚@@@Fast tempos, loud and distorted riffs, simple songs, frequent use of power chords, and shouted vocals, originating in the mid-1970s in response to commercial, sentimental, and complex Rock.@@@快节奏、响亮且失真的连复段、简单的歌曲、频繁使用强力和弦以及呐喊式演唱，起源于20世纪70年代中期，是对商业化、感伤化和复杂化摇滚乐的回应。
Rap Rock@@说唱摇滚@@@Fusion of Hip Hop and Rock, often featuring the former’s rhythmic vocal delivery and the latter’s heavy, distorted instrumentation.@@@嘻哈与摇滚的融合，通常结合了前者的节奏化说唱和后者的沉重、失真的器乐演奏。
Reggae Rock@@雷鬼摇滚@@@Incorporates the offbeat rhythms and staccato "skank" guitar of Reggae.@@@融入了雷鬼音乐的切分节奏和断奏式的"斯卡"吉他风格。

Rock & Roll@@摇滚乐@@@Developed in the United States and popularized in the 1950s, featuring prominent Blues scales, poppy structures, and strong rhythms.@@@在美国发展并于20世纪50年代流行起来，特点是突出的布鲁斯音阶、流行化的结构以及强烈的节奏感。
Rock Musical@@摇滚音乐剧@@@Style of musical theatre inspired by Rock.@@@受摇滚乐启发的音乐剧风格。
Rock Opera@@摇滚歌剧@@@Hybrid of rock and, sometimes, musical theatre stylings with heavily rock-tinged songs combining to tell a coherent story.@@@摇滚乐与（有时是）音乐剧风格的混合体，用带有浓厚摇滚色彩的歌曲组合来讲述一个连贯的故事。
Rock andaluz@@安达卢西亚摇滚@@@Emerged during the mid-1970s in Spain; primarily utilizes Flamenco and often Symphonic Prog elements.@@@兴起于20世纪70年代中期的西班牙；主要运用弗拉门戈元素，并常包含交响前卫摇滚元素。

Rock andino@@安第斯摇滚@@@Emerged in the Andean countries in the late 1960s and early 1970s, incorporating traditional instruments and harmonies from Indigenous Andean Music within a Rock format.@@@兴起于20世纪60年代末70年代初的安第斯国家，在摇滚乐框架内融入了安第斯原住民音乐的传统乐器和和声。

Roots Rock@@根源摇滚@@@Consciously and predominantly incorporates elements of its predecessors and original roots such as Country, Blues, Rhythm & Blues, Rock & Roll, and American Folk Music, but also many other American traditions.@@@有意识地、主要地融合了其前身和根源音乐的元素，如乡村音乐、布鲁斯、节奏布鲁斯、摇滚乐和美国民谣，但也包含许多其他美国传统音乐元素。

Southern Rock@@南方摇滚@@@Emerged from the American South in the early 1970s, blending Blues Rock, Hard Rock, and Rock & Roll with elements of Country and Contemporary Folk.@@@20世纪70年代初兴起于美国南部，融合了布鲁斯摇滚、硬摇滚和摇滚乐，并带有乡村音乐和当代民谣的元素。

Sufi Rock@@苏菲摇滚@@@Pakistani subgenre rooted in Sufi tradition, featuring South Asian instruments like the dhol, tabla, and sitar within a Rock band format with lyrics and imagery inspired by traditional Sufi poets.@@@巴基斯坦音乐子流派，植根于苏菲派传统，融合南亚乐器如多霍尔鼓、塔布拉鼓和西塔琴，采用摇滚乐队形式，歌词与意象灵感源自传统苏菲派诗人。
Surf Music@@冲浪音乐@@@Evolved from late 1950s instrumental Rock & Roll in the USA, particularly associated with the surfing culture in Southern California.@@@起源于20世纪50年代末美国的器乐摇滚乐，尤其与南加州的冲浪文化相关。
Symphonic Rock@@交响摇滚@@@Incorporates elements resembling Orchestral Music.@@@融合了类似管弦乐的元素。
Tolai Rock@@托莱摇滚@@@Fuses the music of the Tolai people of Papua New Guinea with Rock.@@@将巴布亚新几内亚托莱人的音乐与摇滚乐融合。

Zolo@@佐罗@@@Retrospective term grouping artists that fuse elements of New Wave and Progressive Rock with a flamboyant, quirky aesthetic and often the energy of Punk.@@@回顾性术语，指将新浪潮和进步摇滚元素与华丽、古怪的美学，以及常有的朋克能量相结合的艺术家群体。
ASMR@@ASMR (自发性知觉经络反应)@@@Recordings intended to induce a pleasurable tingling sensation in the listener.@@@旨在为听者诱发愉悦的酥麻感的录音。
Jingles@@广告短歌@@@Short, melodic phrases, traditionally used in advertisements, and later in ringtones and video games.@@@短小、旋律化的乐句，传统上用于广告，后来用于手机铃声和电子游戏。

Mechanical Music@@机械音乐@@@Generated by self-playing devices, which read and reproduce music through purely mechanical means with no major electronically controlled elements.@@@由自动演奏设备生成，通过纯机械方式读取和再现音乐，没有主要的电子控制元件。
Children's Music_Misc@@儿童音乐_其它@@@Recorded for an intended audience of children.@@@以为儿童为目标听众而录制。
Fairy Tales@@童话@@@Recorded for children with or without background music.@@@为儿童录制，可有可无背景音乐。
Lullabies@@摇篮曲@@@Soothing, melodic songs that are typically used to help young children sleep.@@@舒缓、旋律优美的歌曲，通常用于帮助幼儿入睡。
Nursery Rhymes@@儿歌@@@Short, mostly traditional children's songs or poems with simple melodies, rhythms and words.@@@简短、多为传统的儿童歌曲或诗歌，旋律、节奏和歌词简单。

Easy Listening_Misc@@轻音乐_其它@@@Typically instrumental, including lush arrangements and melodies played by orchestras or big bands aiming for light entertainment.@@@通常为器乐曲，包含由管弦乐团或大乐队演奏的丰富编曲和旋律，旨在提供轻松的娱乐。
Cocktail Nation@@鸡尾酒国度@@@Decidedly ironic take on Lounge that emerged in the early 1990s.@@@对休闲音乐的一种明显带有讽刺意味的演绎，出现于1990年代初。

Exotica@@异国情调@@@Emerged in the early 1950s as the United States developed a fascination with Polynesia and the South Pacific, incorporates traditional instruments and rhythms from these cultures into a Western Pop context.@@@出现于1950年代初，当时美国对波利尼西亚和南太平洋地区产生了浓厚兴趣，将来自这些文化的传统乐器和节奏融入西方流行乐语境中。

Light Music@@轻音乐@@@Instrumental, typically through-composed style performed by an orchestra or small ensemble, with simpler arrangements and an emphasis on strong melodies.@@@器乐曲，通常为通谱体风格，由管弦乐团或小型合奏团演奏，编曲较为简单，强调强烈的旋律性。

Lounge@@沙发音乐@@@Emerged in the United States during the 1950s, emphasizing a chilled-out atmosphere with light instrumentation and sometimes vocals indebted to Vocal Jazz.@@@出现于1950年代的美国，强调轻松惬意的氛围，配器轻盈，有时人声唱法借鉴自爵士人声。
Pops Orchestra@@流行管弦乐@@@Orchestra that plays covers of popular songs as well as famous classical compositions.@@@演奏流行歌曲以及著名古典作品改编曲的管弦乐团。

Space Age Pop@@太空时代流行@@@Inspired by the excitement and optimism of the Space Age during the 1950s and 1960s, frequently experimenting with futuristic sounds made possible by contemporary technology.@@@灵感源自1950至1960年代太空时代的兴奋与乐观精神，经常尝试利用当代技术创造未来主义的声音。

Experimental_Misc@@实验音乐_其它@@@Most radical approaches throughout history, featuring non-traditional production methods resulting in sounds and styles that go largely beyond traditional boundaries.@@@历史上最激进的手法，采用非传统的制作方法，产生的声音和风格在很大程度上超越了传统界限。
Conducted Improvisation@@指挥即兴@@@Collective improvisation directed by a conductor.@@@由指挥引导的集体即兴创作。
Data Sonification@@数据声化@@@Usage of data to create sound, soundscapes and music mostly as a way to illustrate its information.@@@使用数据来创造声音、声景和音乐，主要作为一种信息呈现方式。
Drone@@嗡鸣音乐@@@Focuses on long, sustained tones and tone clusters, or repetition of single notes.@@@专注于长持续音、音簇或单音的重复。
Electroacoustic@@电子原声@@@Manipulation and processing of sound sources to create atmospheric pieces.@@@通过对声源进行操控和处理来创作氛围音乐作品。

Free Improvisation@@自由即兴@@@Improvised music which, in descending from Free Jazz and classical Indeterminacy, further abandons the prescriptions of harmonic or rhythmic structures which characterized those genres.@@@即兴音乐，源自自由爵士与古典不确定性音乐，进一步摒弃了这些流派标志性的和声或节奏结构规范。

Futurism@@未来主义@@@Radical and militant attitude, rejection of Western Classical Music and traditional criteria of craft, non-conformity, and a fascination with machines.@@@激进且富有战斗性的态度，拒绝西方古典音乐与传统技艺标准，不墨守成规，并对机器着迷。
Glitch@@故障电子@@@Uses abstract sound design and malfunctioning electronics—CD skips, data corruption, and hardware noise—as main compositional elements.@@@以抽象的声音设计和故障电子音效——如CD跳轨、数据损坏和硬件噪音——作为主要的创作元素。

Graphical Sound@@图形声音@@@Compositional technique in which markings are manually applied to a medium, then processed by a machine to produce synthetic sound from the markings in real time.@@@一种作曲技法，将标记手动施加于媒介上，然后由机器处理，实时根据标记生成合成声音。
Indeterminacy@@不确定性音乐@@@Leaves elements of composition, performance, or both up to chance.@@@将作曲、演奏或两者的元素交由偶然性决定。

Industrial@@工业音乐@@@Emerged out of Punk and avant-garde art movements in the mid-1970s, often characterized by a bleak, mechanical atmosphere, the use of metallic percussion as well as other unconventional instruments, and extensive use of sampling influenced by Musique concrète.@@@兴起于20世纪70年代中期的朋克与前卫艺术运动，常以阴郁、机械的氛围，使用金属打击乐及其他非常规乐器，以及受具体音乐影响的广泛采样为特征。
Micromontage@@微蒙太奇@@@Arrangement of microsounds on an incredibly small time scale.@@@在极其微小的时间尺度上安排微声音。
Modern Creative@@现代创意@@@Style of semi-improvisation that derives heavily from both the composition of Modern Classical and the techniques of Avant-Garde Jazz.@@@一种半即兴风格，深受现代古典音乐作曲和前卫爵士乐技法的影响。
Musique concrète instrumentale@@器乐具体音乐@@@Acoustic, through-composed Classical Music that relies heavily on unconventional instrumental techniques.@@@原声、通谱创作的古典音乐，极度依赖非常规的乐器演奏技巧。
Noise@@噪音音乐@@@Strays away from conventional structure, tonality, and composition and consists predominantly of noise.@@@偏离传统的结构、调性与作曲方式，主要由噪音构成。
Plunderphonics@@掠夺性采样@@@Compositional technique of utilizing and manipulating one or more recognizable recordings to create a new composition.@@@一种作曲技法，利用并操控一个或多个可识别的录音来创作新的作品。
Reductionism@@还原主义@@@Experimental music that centers around quiet dynamics and use of silence.@@@以安静动态和运用静默为核心的实验音乐。
Sound Art@@声音艺术@@@Incorporates elements from Experimental music and visual arts, with sound utilized as its primary medium and subject.@@@融合了实验音乐与视觉艺术的元素，将声音作为其主要媒介和主题。
Sound Collage@@声音拼贴@@@Compositional method emphasizing the rough layering of sampled elements, presenting an impression similar to a visual collage.@@@强调采样元素粗粝叠加的作曲方法，呈现出类似视觉拼贴画的印象。
Sound Poetry@@声音诗歌@@@Emphasis is placed upon phonetics and the sounds of syllables rather than the meaning of the words themselves.@@@重点在于语音和音节的声音，而非词语本身的含义。
Tape Music@@磁带音乐@@@Explores the nature of audio recorded to magnetic tape and the act of playing sound from it.@@@探索录制到磁带上的音频的本质，以及从中播放声音这一行为。

Turntable Music@@唱盘音乐@@@Has its roots in the early half of the twentieth century, with experimental artists using the turntable (as well as the vinyl records it plays) as a distinct instrument in the creation of compositions.@@@其根源可追溯到二十世纪上半叶，实验艺术家将唱机（及其播放的黑胶唱片）作为一种独特的乐器用于创作。
Field Recordings_Misc@@田野录音_其它@@@Material recorded outside of a recording studio for the purpose of capturing the audible illustration of an environment.@@@在录音室外录制，旨在捕捉环境声音的素材。
Nature Recordings@@自然录音@@@Recordings of natural phenomena such as waterfalls, ocean waves, and storms, as well as Animal Sounds.@@@记录自然现象的声音，如瀑布、海浪、风暴，以及动物叫声。
Radio Broadcast Recordings@@广播录音@@@Recordings of transmissions sent by radio waves, used for sending communications to a wide audience.@@@通过无线电波发送的广播录音，用于向广大受众传递信息。
Scenes & Movements_Misc@@场景与运动_其它@@@@@@
Afrofuturism@@非洲未来主义@@@Movement rooted in Jazz and Funk, blending futuristic aesthetics and themes with African and Afrodiasporic culture.@@@植根于爵士乐和放克乐的运动，融合未来主义美学和主题与非洲及非洲离散文化。
Akron Sound@@阿克伦之声@@@Early Punk and New Wave scene in Akron, Ohio and nearby Kent from the mid-1970s to the early 1980s.@@@20世纪70年代中期至80年代初，俄亥俄州阿克伦市及附近肯特市的早期朋克和新浪潮场景。

Alté@@Alternative，融合的慵懒音乐风格@@@More subdued, atmospheric, minimalist, and eclectic production and vocal styles compared to earlier styles, as well as more socially conscious lyrics.@@@与早期风格相比，制作和演唱风格更内敛、氛围化、极简且兼收并蓄，歌词也更关注社会议题。
Anarcho-Punk@@无政府朋克@@@Intentionally rougher and sloppier sound, with lyrics pertaining to anarchist ideology and sociopolitical injustices.@@@刻意追求更粗糙、更随意的声音，歌词涉及无政府主义意识形态和社会政治不公。

Asian Underground@@亚洲地下@@@South Asian diasporic movement defined by musical acts, producers and DJs fusing contemporary Electronic music with Bhangra and other traditional styles.@@@南亚离散文化运动，由音乐人、制作人和DJ将当代电子音乐与邦拉及其他传统风格融合所定义。
Aussie Pub Rock@@澳洲酒吧摇滚@@@Straightforward Rock acts associated with the Australian pub circuit of the 1970s and 1980s.@@@与20世纪70、80年代澳大利亚酒吧巡回演出相关的直率摇滚乐队。

Authenticité@@真实主义@@@Post-independence cultural movement born in socialist Guinea aiming to reclaim pre-colonial heritage and forge a shared national identity, largely through government-funded programs, national orchestras, and radical rejection of European influences.@@@几内亚独立后兴起的文化运动，旨在社会主义几内亚的背景下，主要通过政府资助项目、国家管弦乐团和彻底摒弃欧洲影响，重拾前殖民遗产并塑造共同的国家认同。
Bay Area Thrash Metal@@湾区敲击金属@@@Highly influential Thrash Metal scene centered around the San Francisco Bay Area.@@@以旧金山湾区为中心，极具影响力的鞭挞金属音乐场景。
Beach Music@@沙滩音乐@@@Danceable 4/4 "blues shuffle" rhythmic structure, vocal harmonies, and moderate tempos.@@@具有可舞动的4/4拍"蓝调曳步"节奏结构、和声演唱以及中等速度。

Beijing New Sound@@北京新声@@@Pioneering Chinese indie Rock movement in the late '90s that renewed the heavy narratives of early '90s Chinese rock with its stylish sound and personal themes.@@@90年代末开创性的中国独立摇滚运动，以其时尚的声响和私人化的主题，复兴了90年代初中国摇滚的沉重叙事。
Bernese Dialect Scene@@伯尔尼方言场景@@@Influential scene of artists centered in the Swiss city of Bern, featuring poetic lyrics in Bernese German.@@@以瑞士伯尔尼市为中心的有影响力的艺术家圈子，其特色是使用伯尔尼德语创作的诗意歌词。

Bloghouse@@博客浩室@@@Internet-based movement from the mid-to-late-2000s which broadly distilled scenes most associated with online music blogging, mp3-sharing and remix culture, intersecting within Dance styles such as Electroclash, Electro House, New Rave and Nu-Disco in a brash and carefree fashion.@@@2000年代中后期兴起的基于互联网的运动，广泛融合了与在线音乐博客、MP3分享和混音文化最相关的场景，并以一种张扬随性的方式与电子克、电子浩室、新锐舞和新迪斯科等舞曲风格相交织。
